items.translation: (It's incredible that nobody noticed the such obvious error before)
items.explanation: Present amazement about past oversight uses imperfect subjunctive. Surprise about past lack of noticing.

items.question: Siento muchísimo que _____ tenido que pasar por esa situación difícil.
items.translation: (I'm very sorry you had to go through that difficult situation)
items.explanation: Present sorrow about past experience uses present perfect subjunctive. Sympathy for completed suffering.

items.question: Me sorprende que todavía no _____ respondido a mi mensaje urgente.
items.translation: (It surprises me you still haven't responded to my urgent message)
items.explanation: Surprise about lack of completion uses present perfect subjunctive. Astonishment at non-action.

items.question: Estoy encantado de que tú _____ participar en nuestro proyecto importante.
items.translation: (I'm delighted that you can participate in our important project)
items.explanation: Delight about capability triggers subjunctive. Positive emotion about ability uses subjunctive.

items.question: Me preocupa que ellos no _____ tomando este asunto con la seriedad necesaria.
items.translation: (It worries me they're not taking this matter with the necessary seriousness)
items.explanation: Worry about current situation triggers subjunctive. Concern about ongoing behavior.

items.question: Es emocionante que finalmente _____ a realizar nuestro sueño compartido.
items.translation: (It's exciting we're finally going to fulfill our shared dream)
items.explanation: Excitement about future plan triggers subjunctive. Emotional reaction to anticipated action.

items.question: Lamento profundamente que las circunstancias no nos _____ reunir antes.
items.translation: (I deeply regret the circumstances didn't allow us to meet earlier)
items.explanation: Present regret about past prevention uses imperfect subjunctive. Sorrow about missed opportunity.

items.question: Me da rabia que siempre _____ excusas en lugar de admitir tus errores.
items.translation: (It makes me angry you always invent excuses instead of admitting your mistakes)
items.explanation: 'Dar rabia' triggers subjunctive for anger cause. Frustration about habitual behavior.

items.question: Es triste que tantas personas _____ sin hogar en ciudades ricas.
items.translation: (It's sad that so many people are homeless in wealthy cities)
items.explanation: Sadness about current reality triggers subjunctive. Emotional reaction to unfortunate fact.

items.question: Me fascinó que ella _____ cinco idiomas con tanta fluidez natural.
items.translation: (It fascinated me that she spoke five languages with such natural fluency)
items.explanation: Past fascination about simultaneous ability uses imperfect subjunctive. Amazement in past about concurrent skill.

items.question: Espero sinceramente que todo _____ salido bien en tu entrevista importante.
items.translation: (I sincerely hope everything went well in your important interview)
items.explanation: Present hope about past outcome uses present perfect subjunctive. Hoping for successful past event.

name: Indirect Commands & Suggestions
description: Que + subjunctive for third person commands

items.question: ¡Que _____ buen viaje y que lo disfruten al máximo!
items.translation: (Have a good trip and enjoy it to the fullest!)
items.explanation: 'Que + subjunctive' for indirect wishes to third person. Wishing for others uses subjunctive.

items.question: ¡Que _____ mucha suerte en su examen final de mañana!
items.translation: (Good luck on your final exam tomorrow!)
items.explanation: 'Que tengas suerte' standard luck wish. Indirect command wishing fortune.

items.question: ¡Que lo _____ muy bien en su nueva aventura profesional!
items.translation: (May they do very well in their new professional adventure!)
items.explanation: 'Que + subjunctive' for wishing well to others. Third person well-wishes.

items.question: ¡Que _____ a casa sano y salvo después del viaje largo!
items.translation: (May he arrive home safe and sound after the long trip!)
items.explanation: Indirect wish for safe arrival uses subjunctive. Hoping for someone's safe journey.

items.question: ¡Que _____ cumpleaños muy feliz rodeado de tu familia!
items.translation: (Have a very happy birthday surrounded by your family!)
items.explanation: 'Que tengas' birthday wish is standard. Celebration wishes use subjunctive.

items.question: ¡Que Dios te _____ siempre en todos tus emprendimientos!
items.translation: (May God bless you always in all your endeavors!)
items.explanation: Religious blessings use subjunctive after 'que'. Divine wishes require subjunctive.

items.question: ¡Que se _____ pronto de su enfermedad y vuelva al trabajo!
items.translation: (May she recover soon from her illness and return to work!)
items.explanation: Health recovery wishes use subjunctive. Hoping for someone's healing.

items.question: ¡Que no _____ nunca lo importantes que son para nosotros!
items.translation: (May they never forget how important they are to us!)
items.explanation: Negative indirect command uses subjunctive. Wishing against forgetting.

items.question: ¡Que _____ todos sus sueños más grandes en la vida!
items.translation: (May all your biggest dreams in life come true!)
items.explanation: Wishes for dream fulfillment use subjunctive. Hoping for realization of aspirations.

items.question: ¡Que te _____ muy bien el nuevo trabajo que acabas de empezar!
items.translation: (May the new job you just started go very well for you!)
items.explanation: 'Que te vaya bien' standard well-wishing. Success wishes use subjunctive.

items.question: ¡Que _____ con mucha salud y felicidad por muchos años más!
items.translation: (May you live with great health and happiness for many more years!)
items.explanation: Longevity wishes use subjunctive. Hoping for long healthy life.

items.question: ¡Que el próximo año _____ mejor que este para todos nosotros!
items.translation: (May next year be better than this one for all of us!)
items.explanation: New year wishes use subjunctive. Hoping for better future.

items.question: ¡Que les _____ todo muy bien en su mudanza a la nueva ciudad!
items.translation: (May everything go very well for them in their move to the new city!)
items.explanation: Wishing success for others' endeavors uses subjunctive. Third person well-wishes.

items.question: ¡Que _____ siempre el amor verdadero en tu corazón!
items.translation: (May true love always remain in your heart!)
items.explanation: Romantic wishes use subjunctive. Hoping for lasting love.

items.question: ¡Que nadie te _____ nunca apagar tu luz interior brillante!
items.translation: (May nobody ever make you dim your bright inner light!)
items.explanation: Protective wishes against harm use subjunctive. Hoping against negative influence.

items.question: Llevo más de dos horas _____ este informe complicado sin parar.
items.translation: (I've been writing this complicated report for over two hours non-stop)
items.explanation: 'Llevar + time + gerund' expresses duration of ongoing action. Emphasizes time invested in continuing activity.

items.question: Ella lleva tres años _____ español y ya habla con mucha fluidez.
items.translation: (She's been studying Spanish for three years and already speaks very fluently)
items.explanation: 'Llevar + time + gerund' shows accumulated experience. Emphasizes ongoing learning over period.

items.question: Voy _____ cada vez más frustrado con esta situación sin resolver.
items.translation: (I'm getting more and more frustrated with this unresolved situation)
items.explanation: 'Ir + gerund' shows gradual change. Progressive increase in emotional state.

items.question: Ando _____ un libro interesante sobre la historia de España.
items.translation: (I'm in the process of reading an interesting book about Spanish history)
items.explanation: 'Andar + gerund' emphasizes ongoing process. More casual than 'estar', shows activity in progress.

items.question: Sigo _____ dificultades con este concepto matemático tan abstracto.
items.translation: (I'm still having difficulties with this very abstract mathematical concept)
items.explanation: 'Seguir + gerund' means continue doing. Emphasizes persistence of problem.

items.question: Continúa _____ a pesar de todos los obstáculos en su camino.
items.translation: (She continues trying despite all the obstacles in her path)
items.explanation: 'Continuar + gerund' emphasizes persistence. Ongoing effort despite difficulties.

items.question: Vengo _____ este tema desde hace varios meses sin éxito.
items.translation: (I've been investigating this topic for several months without success)
items.explanation: 'Venir + gerund' shows action coming from past to present. Extended ongoing research.

items.question: El clima va _____ cada año más cálido en nuestro planeta.
items.translation: (The climate is getting warmer every year on our planet)
items.explanation: 'Ir + gerund' for gradual ongoing change. Progressive climate change over time.

items.question: Llevo todo el día _____ llamarte pero nunca contestas el teléfono.
items.translation: (I've been trying to call you all day but you never answer the phone)
items.explanation: 'Llevar + time + gerund' for repeated attempts. Emphasizes duration of unsuccessful tries.

items.question: Anda _____ de que está enfermo para no ir al trabajo.
items.translation: (He's going around saying he's sick so he doesn't have to go to work)
items.explanation: 'Andar + gerund' for spreading information. Ongoing talking/claiming.

items.question: Vamos _____ cada vez mejores resultados con este nuevo método.
items.translation: (We're getting better and better results with this new method)
items.explanation: 'Ir + gerund' shows progressive improvement. Gradual increase in success.

items.question: Quedó _____ en que nos encontraríamos mañana en la cafetería.
items.translation: (We agreed that we would meet tomorrow at the café)
items.explanation: 'Quedar en' doesn't use gerund. Exception: this verb means 'agree' without progressive.

items.question: Lleva años _____ en ese proyecto sin ver resultados concretos todavía.
items.translation: (He's been working on that project for years without seeing concrete results yet)
items.explanation: 'Llevar + years + gerund' emphasizes long duration. Extended time on incomplete project.

items.question: Sigue _____ lo mismo de siempre sin cambiar nada en absoluto.
items.translation: (You keep doing the same as always without changing anything at all)
items.explanation: 'Seguir + gerund' for continued unchanged behavior. Persistent repetition.

items.question: Vengo _____ este problema durante semanas y no encuentro solución.
items.translation: (I've been thinking about this problem for weeks and can't find a solution)
items.explanation: 'Venir + gerund' for extended pondering. Ongoing mental effort from past to now.

items.question: Yo _____ ido contigo si hubiera tenido tiempo libre disponible.
items.translation: (I would have gone with you if I had had free time available)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect expresses unrealized past possibility. What would have happened in different circumstances.

items.question: Ella nunca _____ dicho eso si hubiera sabido toda la verdad.
items.translation: (She never would have said that if she had known the whole truth)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect with 'nunca' for emphasis. Hypothetical past action prevented by lack of knowledge.

items.question: ¿Qué _____ hecho tú en mi lugar en esa situación tan complicada?
items.translation: (What would you have done in my place in that very complicated situation?)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect in questions about hypothetical past. Asking about alternative actions.

items.question: Ellos _____ llegado más temprano si no hubiera habido tanto tráfico.
items.translation: (They would have arrived earlier if there hadn't been so much traffic)
items.explanation: Third person plural conditional perfect. Past hypothetical prevented by circumstances.

items.question: Nosotros _____ comprado esa casa si hubiéramos tenido suficiente dinero.
items.translation: (We would have bought that house if we had had enough money)
items.explanation: First person plural conditional perfect. Missed opportunity due to financial constraint.

items.question: Yo te _____ avisado antes si hubiera sabido que venías hoy.
items.translation: (I would have warned you earlier if I had known you were coming today)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect for failed notification. Lack of information prevented action.

items.question: Sin tu ayuda valiosa, nunca _____ terminado este proyecto a tiempo.
items.translation: (Without your valuable help, I never would have finished this project on time)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect showing dependence on help. Acknowledging crucial assistance.

items.question: Ella _____ estudiado medicina si sus padres no se hubieran opuesto.
items.translation: (She would have studied medicine if her parents hadn't opposed it)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect for unrealized career path. Parental opposition changed outcome.

items.question: Ellos nunca _____ imaginado que el proyecto tendría tanto éxito.
items.translation: (They never would have imagined the project would have so much success)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect for surprise at outcome. Unexpected success beyond imagination.

items.question: Yo _____ estado muy feliz de acompañarte en ese viaje inolvidable.
items.translation: (I would have been very happy to accompany you on that unforgettable trip)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect for hypothetical past happiness. Missed opportunity for joy.

items.question: ¿Tú _____ tomado la misma decisión difícil en mi situación?
items.translation: (Would you have made the same difficult decision in my situation?)
items.explanation: Question in conditional perfect seeks opinion. Asking about hypothetical past choice.

items.question: Nosotros _____ preferido que nos consultaran antes de decidir todo.
items.translation: (We would have preferred that they consult us before deciding everything)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect expressing unmet preference. Wish for different past consultation.

items.question: Él _____ sido un gran músico si hubiera practicado más de joven.
items.translation: (He would have been a great musician if he had practiced more when young)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect for unrealized potential. Talent unrealized due to insufficient practice.

items.question: Yo nunca _____ creído esta historia increíble si no la viera con mis ojos.
items.translation: (I never would have believed this incredible story if I didn't see it with my eyes)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect for skepticism overcome by evidence. Disbelief changed by witness.

items.question: Ellas _____ venido a la fiesta si no hubieran estado tan ocupadas.
items.translation: (They would have come to the party if they hadn't been so busy)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect for prevented attendance. Busyness stopped party participation.

items.question: A mi madre le _____ muchísimo las películas románticas de época.
items.translation: (My mother loves period romantic movies very much)
items.explanation: 'Encantar' stronger than gustar, follows same pattern. Plural 'encantan' for plural 'películas'.

items.question: Me _____ los pies después de caminar todo el día por la ciudad.
items.translation: (My feet hurt after walking all day through the city)
items.explanation: 'Doler' follows gustar pattern with plural subject. Plural 'duelen' for plural 'pies'.

items.question: A los estudiantes les _____ tres libros más para completar el curso.
items.translation: (The students need three more books to complete the course)
items.explanation: 'Faltar' follows gustar with plural subject. Plural 'faltan' for plural books needed.

items.question: ¿A ti te _____ bien estos pantalones nuevos que acabo de comprar?
items.translation: (Do these new pants I just bought fit you well?)
items.explanation: 'Quedar' for clothing fit follows gustar. Plural 'quedan' for plural pants.

items.question: A nosotros nos _____ viajar durante el invierno por el clima frío.
items.translation: (We feel like traveling during winter because of the cold weather)
items.explanation: 'Apetecer' means appeal to/feel like. Singular for infinitive 'viajar'.

items.question: Me _____ muy mal cuando la gente no cumple sus promesas.
items.translation: (It rubs me the wrong way when people don't keep their promises)
items.explanation: 'Caer mal' means dislike/rub wrong way. Negative feeling about people.

items.question: A mi hermano le _____ todas las materias científicas en la escuela.
items.translation: (All scientific subjects at school interest my brother)
items.explanation: 'Interesar' follows gustar pattern. Plural 'interesan' for plural subjects.

items.question: ¿Te _____ esos zapatos elegantes que vi en la tienda ayer?
items.translation: (Do you like those elegant shoes I saw in the store yesterday?)
items.explanation: 'Gustar' with plural subject needs plural verb. 'Zapatos' plural requires 'gustan'.

items.question: A mis padres les _____ mucho cocinar juntos los fines de semana.
items.translation: (My parents really enjoy cooking together on weekends)
items.explanation: 'Gustar' with infinitive takes singular. Activity 'cocinar' is singular concept.

items.question: Me _____ extraño que no hayas llamado en toda la semana.
items.translation: (It seems strange to me you haven't called all week)
items.explanation: 'Parecer' follows gustar for seeming. Singular for que-clause.

items.question: A ella le _____ fatal levantarse temprano durante la semana laboral.
items.translation: (She hates getting up early during the work week)
items.explanation: 'Sentar fatal' means dislike intensely. Stronger negative than 'no gustar'.

items.question: Nos _____ solo dos días para terminar completamente este proyecto grande.
items.translation: (We have only two days left to completely finish this big project)
items.explanation: 'Quedar' for remaining time. Plural 'quedan' for plural days.

items.question: Me _____ muchísimo esa actitud negativa que tienes últimamente.
items.translation: (That negative attitude you have lately bothers me a lot)
items.explanation: 'Molestar' for annoyance. Singular 'molesta' for singular 'actitud'.

items.question: A los niños les _____ jugar videojuegos durante horas sin parar.
items.translation: (Children love playing video games for hours without stopping)
items.explanation: 'Encantar' with infinitive takes singular. Activity is singular concept.

items.question: ¿Te _____ bien ese nuevo compañero de trabajo que llegó?
items.translation: (Do you like that new coworker who arrived?)
items.explanation: 'Caer bien' for liking people. Person impression, singular 'cae'.

items.question: No quiero café _____ té verde con miel y limón.
items.translation: (I don't want coffee but rather green tea with honey and lemon)
items.explanation: 'Sino' corrects negative statement. After 'no quiero', use 'sino' to state what you do want.

items.question: Es inteligente _____ muy perezoso para estudiar con dedicación.
items.translation: (He's intelligent but very lazy to study with dedication)
items.explanation: 'Pero' contrasts two qualities. Both adjectives can coexist, just contrasting.

items.question: No solo es talentoso _____ también muy trabajador y disciplinado.
items.translation: (Not only is he talented but also very hardworking and disciplined)
items.explanation: 'No solo...sino también' pattern. Adding positive quality after initial one.

items.question: No me llamó _____ me mandó un mensaje de texto largo.
items.translation: (He didn't call me but rather sent me a long text message)
items.explanation: 'Sino que' before conjugated verb. Correcting negative statement with verb clause.

items.question: El examen era difícil _____ logré aprobar con buena nota.
items.translation: (The exam was difficult but I managed to pass with a good grade)
items.explanation: 'Pero' for contrasting statements. Difficulty doesn't contradict passing.

items.question: No es francesa _____ belga, aunque habla perfectamente francés.
items.translation: (She's not French but rather Belgian, although she speaks French perfectly)
items.explanation: 'Sino' corrects nationality assumption. After negative identity, state correct one.

items.question: Estudia mucho _____ no consigue sacar buenas calificaciones todavía.
items.translation: (She studies a lot but doesn't manage to get good grades yet)
items.explanation: 'Pero' shows contrary result. Effort doesn't guarantee success.

items.question: No terminamos el proyecto ayer _____ lo terminaremos hoy sin falta.
items.translation: (We didn't finish the project yesterday but rather we'll finish it today without fail)
items.explanation: 'Sino que' with conjugated verb. Correcting when completion happened.

items.question: Quiere viajar _____ no tiene suficiente dinero ahorrado ahora.
items.translation: (He wants to travel but doesn't have enough money saved now)
items.explanation: 'Pero' contrasts desire with reality. Wanting something despite obstacle.

items.question: No es que no quiera ayudarte _____ que no tengo tiempo libre.
items.translation: (It's not that I don't want to help you but rather that I don't have free time)
items.explanation: 'No es que...sino que' corrects assumption. Clarifying true reason.

items.question: Habla bien español _____ todavía comete algunos errores gramaticales.
items.translation: (She speaks Spanish well but still makes some grammatical errors)
items.explanation: 'Pero' acknowledges imperfection. Good ability doesn't mean perfection.

items.question: No fue un error _____ una decisión consciente y bien pensada.
items.translation: (It wasn't a mistake but rather a conscious and well-thought decision)
items.explanation: 'Sino' corrects characterization. Defining something as opposite of assumption.

items.question: No vino porque estaba enfermo _____ porque no tenía ganas.
items.translation: (He didn't come not because he was sick but because he didn't feel like it)
items.explanation: 'Sino' corrects reason given. True cause different from stated one.

items.question: Trabajo mucho _____ también necesito tiempo para relajarme y descansar.
items.translation: (I work a lot but I also need time to relax and rest)
items.explanation: 'Pero' adds consideration. Work doesn't eliminate need for rest.

items.question: No lo hago por obligación _____ porque realmente me gusta mucho.
items.translation: (I don't do it out of obligation but rather because I really like it a lot)
items.explanation: 'Sino' corrects motivation. True reason opposes assumed one.

name: Modal Verbs - Advanced Uses
description: Poder, deber, querer in complex contexts

items.question: Deberías _____ visitado a tu abuela cuando estuvo enferma en el hospital.
items.translation: (You should have visited your grandmother when she was sick in the hospital)
items.explanation: Conditional + haber + past participle for past obligation. 'Deberías haber' expresses regret about unmet duty.

items.question: No puede _____ que ella no sepa nada sobre este importante asunto.
items.translation: (It can't be that she doesn't know anything about this important matter)
items.explanation: 'No puede ser que' expresses disbelief. Impossibility of situation requires 'ser'.

items.question: Pudiera _____ que estén equivocados en sus conclusiones apresuradas.
items.translation: (It could be they're wrong in their hasty conclusions)
items.explanation: 'Pudiera ser que' expresses possibility. Imperfect subjunctive 'pudiera' softens suggestion.

items.question: Habría _____ llegar más temprano si hubiera sabido sobre el tráfico.
items.translation: (I should have arrived earlier if I had known about the traffic)
items.explanation: Conditional perfect 'habría debido' for past obligation. Recognizing what should have been done.

items.question: ¿Podrías _____ el favor de cerrar esa ventana que está abierta?
items.translation: (Could you do me the favor of closing that window that's open?)
items.explanation: '¿Podrías hacerme el favor?' polite request formula. Standard courteous asking pattern.

items.question: Debe de _____ muy caro vivir en esa zona tan exclusiva de la ciudad.
items.translation: (It must be very expensive to live in that very exclusive area of the city)
items.explanation: 'Debe de' expresses supposition/probability. 'De' indicates conjecture rather than obligation.

items.question: No quisiera _____ grosero, pero tu propuesta tiene varios problemas.
items.translation: (I wouldn't want to be rude, but your proposal has several problems)
items.explanation: 'No quisiera ser' for polite disagreement. Conditional softens potentially offensive statement.

items.question: Pueden _____ terminado el trabajo si hubieran empezado más temprano.
items.translation: (They could have finished the work if they had started earlier)
items.explanation: 'Pueden haber' + past participle for past possibility. Potential completion prevented by late start.

items.question: Deberías de _____ más cuidado con tus pertenencias personales valiosas.
items.translation: (You should be more careful with your valuable personal belongings)
items.explanation: 'Deber de' can express advice. Though 'deber' alone is more common, both work.

items.question: No podemos _____ esperado un resultado mejor dadas las circunstancias.
items.translation: (We couldn't have expected a better result given the circumstances)
items.explanation: 'No poder haber' + past participle. Impossibility of past expectation.

items.question: Querría _____ una consulta sobre los servicios que ustedes ofrecen.
items.translation: (I would like to make an inquiry about the services you offer)
items.explanation: 'Querría hacer' very polite request form. Conditional 'querría' softer than 'quiero'.

items.question: Debiste _____ dicho la verdad desde el principio del asunto.
items.translation: (You should have told the truth from the beginning of the matter)
items.explanation: 'Debiste haber' for strong past obligation. Criticism of past failure to act.

items.question: Pudiste _____ evitado todo este lío si hubieras sido más honesto.
items.translation: (You could have avoided all this mess if you had been more honest)
items.explanation: 'Pudiste haber' for missed past opportunity. Avoidable problem due to dishonesty.

items.question: No quiso _____ sus verdaderos motivos para tomar esa decisión drástica.
items.translation: (He didn't want to reveal his true motives for making that drastic decision)
items.explanation: 'Querer' + infinitive for unwillingness. Preterite 'quiso' for specific refusal.

items.question: Habría _____ ayudarte más si me hubieras explicado mejor la situación.
items.translation: (I would have been able to help you more if you had explained the situation better)
items.explanation: 'Habría podido' for past potential. Better explanation would have enabled more help.

items.question: El médico con _____ hablé ayer me recomendó cambiar mi dieta completamente.
items.translation: (The doctor with whom I spoke yesterday recommended I change my diet completely)
items.explanation: After prepositions referring to people, use 'quien/quienes'. Professional contexts need formal relative pronouns.

items.question: La razón por _____ renuncié al trabajo era el ambiente tóxico laboral.
items.translation: (The reason for which I resigned from the job was the toxic work environment)
items.explanation: After 'por' with abstract feminine noun, 'la cual' or 'la que' work. 'Cual' is more formal.

items.question: Esos son los colegas en _____ confío plenamente para este proyecto.
items.translation: (Those are the colleagues in whom I fully trust for this project)
items.explanation: After preposition with plural people, use 'quienes'. Professional trust requires formal structure.

items.question: La empresa para _____ trabajo ofrece excelentes beneficios a empleados.
items.translation: (The company for which I work offers excellent benefits to employees)
items.explanation: After 'para' with organization, use 'la que/la cual'. Both are acceptable.

items.question: El estudiante _____ trabajo recibió la nota más alta es mi mejor amigo.
items.translation: (The student whose paper received the highest grade is my best friend)
items.explanation: 'Cuyo' means 'whose' and agrees with possessed thing. Here 'cuyo' (masculine) agrees with 'trabajo'.

items.question: _____ más me impresionó fue su increíble dedicación al proyecto difícil.
items.translation: (What impressed me most was his incredible dedication to the difficult project)
items.explanation: 'Lo que' at sentence start means 'what'. Refers to abstract concept or whole idea.

items.question: La manera en _____ resolvió el problema demostró su gran inteligencia.
items.translation: (The way in which he solved the problem demonstrated his great intelligence)
items.explanation: After 'manera' use 'en la que/la cual'. More formal than just 'como'.

items.question: Aquellos _____ deseen participar deben inscribirse antes del viernes que viene.
items.translation: (Those who wish to participate must register before next Friday)
items.explanation: 'Aquellos que' for 'those who' in formal announcements. Simple 'que' after demonstrative.

items.question: El edificio en _____ vivimos tiene más de cien años de antigüedad.
items.translation: (The building in which we live is over one hundred years old)
items.explanation: After 'en' with buildings, use 'el que/el cual'. Though 'donde' also works.

items.question: La profesora _____ clases son las más interesantes ganó un premio.
items.translation: (The professor whose classes are the most interesting won an award)
items.explanation: 'Cuyas' (feminine plural) agrees with 'clases'. Possession relative pronoun must agree.

items.question: Todo _____ necesitas para triunfar ya está dentro de ti mismo.
items.translation: (Everything you need to succeed is already inside yourself)
items.explanation: 'Todo lo que' for 'everything that'. 'Lo que' required after 'todo'.

items.question: Los documentos sin _____ no podemos proceder están en camino.
items.translation: (The documents without which we cannot proceed are on their way)
items.explanation: After 'sin' with things, use 'los cuales/los que'. Formal business language.

items.question: Esa es la chica de _____ te hablé tanto la semana pasada.
items.translation: (That's the girl about whom I talked to you so much last week)
items.explanation: After preposition referring to person, 'quien' is standard. Talking about someone specific.

items.question: El momento en _____ tomé esa decisión cambió completamente mi vida.
items.translation: (The moment in which I made that decision completely changed my life)
items.explanation: Time moments use 'en el que/el cual'. Though 'cuando' works, 'en el que' is more formal.

items.question: Personas _____ nunca han viajado al extranjero tienen visión limitada.
items.translation: (People who have never traveled abroad have a limited vision)
items.explanation: 'Que' simplest for restrictive clauses about people. Most natural choice.

items.question: Tengo el placer de _____ que su solicitud ha sido aprobada.
items.translation: (I have the pleasure of informing you that your application has been approved)
items.explanation: 'Comunicar' most formal for business announcements. Professional correspondence requires formal verbs.

items.question: Le _____ adjunto el informe trimestral que me solicitó la semana pasada.
items.translation: (I'm sending you attached the quarterly report you requested last week)
items.explanation: 'Enviar adjunto' standard business phrase. Professional email language.

items.question: Agradeceríamos que nos _____ su respuesta antes del próximo viernes.
items.translation: (We would appreciate if you could give us your response before next Friday)
items.explanation: 'Hacer llegar' very formal business expression. Ultra-professional request phrasing.

items.question: Nos _____ en informarle que el pedido ha sido procesado exitosamente.
items.translation: (We are pleased to inform you the order has been successfully processed)
items.explanation: 'Complacer' very formal pleasure expression. Business satisfaction statement.

items.question: Le ruego que _____ la presente como confirmación oficial del acuerdo.
items.translation: (I ask you to consider this letter as official confirmation of the agreement)
items.explanation: 'Rogar que' formal request with subjunctive. Business letters use 'rogar' for polite asking.

items.question: Me _____ poner a su disposición para cualquier consulta adicional.
items.translation: (I am pleased to make myself available for any additional questions)
items.explanation: 'Me es grato' ultra-formal pleasure phrase. Business correspondence closing.

items.question: Quedamos _____ la espera de sus gratas noticias al respecto.
items.translation: (We remain awaiting your favorable news regarding this matter)
items.explanation: 'Quedar a la espera' formal waiting expression. Business letter standard closing.

items.question: Sin otro _____, aprovecho la ocasión para saludarle atentamente.
items.translation: (Without further matter, I take this opportunity to greet you respectfully)
items.explanation: 'Sin otro particular' archaic formal closing. Traditional business letter ending.

items.question: Nos _____ mucho en disculparnos por las molestias ocasionadas.
items.translation: (We are very sorry to apologize for the inconveniences caused)
items.explanation: 'Nos complacemos en' formal apology introduction. Professional regret expression.

items.question: Tengo el honor de _____ a la inauguración de nuestras nuevas oficinas.
items.translation: (I have the honor of inviting you to the inauguration of our new offices)
items.explanation: 'Invitar' formal for business events. 'Le' maintains formal tone.

items.question: Le _____ que se comunique con nuestro departamento de atención al cliente.
items.translation: (We request that you contact our customer service department)
items.explanation: 'Rogar' most polite formal request. Business courtesy language.

items.question: Me _____ en agradecerle su valiosa colaboración en este proyecto importante.
items.translation: (I am pleased to thank you for your valuable collaboration on this important project)
items.explanation: 'Me complazco en' ultra-formal gratitude. Professional thank you phrasing.

items.question: _____ a su conocimiento que la reunión ha sido pospuesta.
items.translation: (We bring to your attention that the meeting has been postponed)
items.explanation: 'Poner a su conocimiento' formal notification phrase. Business announcement language.

items.question: Le saluda _____ y queda a su entera disposición para servir.
items.translation: (Greets you respectfully and remains at your complete disposal to serve)
items.explanation: 'Atentamente' most formal business greeting. Standard professional salutation.

items.question: _____ acusar recibo de su comunicación del pasado día quince.
items.translation: (We acknowledge receipt of your communication of the fifteenth)
items.explanation: 'Acusar recibo' archaic formal acknowledgment. Traditional business confirmation phrase.

name: Literary Subjunctive
description: Subjunctive in literature and formal writing

items.question: Ojalá que este sueño maravilloso nunca _____.
items.translation: (If only this wonderful dream would never end)
items.explanation: 'Ojalá que' always triggers subjunctive. Expressing fervent wishes about present or future.

items.question: Tal vez _____ la respuesta que buscamos en lugares inesperados.
items.translation: (Perhaps we'll find the answer we seek in unexpected places)
items.explanation: 'Tal vez' can use subjunctive or indicative. Subjunctive emphasizes uncertainty more.

items.question: Quizás _____ demasiado tarde para cambiar el destino que nos espera.
items.translation: (Perhaps it's too late to change the destiny that awaits us)
items.explanation: 'Quizás' with subjunctive emphasizes doubt. Literary uncertainty prefers subjunctive.

items.question: Aunque me _____ la vida entera, jamás olvidaré ese momento mágico.
items.translation: (Even if I live my whole life, I'll never forget that magical moment)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with hypothetical concession uses subjunctive. Literary emphasis on hypothetical duration.

items.question: Quienquiera que _____, tendrá que enfrentar las graves consecuencias.
items.translation: (Whoever it may be, will have to face the serious consequences)
items.explanation: 'Quienquiera que' (whoever) always requires subjunctive. Unknown identity needs subjunctive.

items.question: Dondequiera que _____, mi corazón siempre estará contigo en espíritu.
items.translation: (Wherever you go, my heart will always be with you in spirit)
items.explanation: 'Dondequiera que' (wherever) requires subjunctive. Indefinite location needs this mood.

items.question: Acaso _____ la verdad escondida detrás de todas estas mentiras.
items.translation: (Perhaps we'll discover the truth hidden behind all these lies)
items.explanation: 'Acaso' literary equivalent of 'quizás'. Subjunctive preferred in literature.

items.question: Sea como _____, debemos aceptar las circunstancias que la vida nos presenta.
items.translation: (Be that as it may, we must accept the circumstances life presents us)
items.explanation: 'Sea como fuere' archaic literary phrase. Fixed expression meaning 'be that as it may'.

items.question: Que yo _____, nadie ha logrado resolver este antiguo misterio todavía.
items.translation: (As far as I know, nobody has managed to solve this ancient mystery yet)
items.explanation: 'Que yo sepa' for 'as far as I know'. Personal knowledge limitation expression.

items.question: Por mucho que _____, la felicidad verdadera siempre parecía inalcanzable.
items.translation: (However much he searched, true happiness always seemed unattainable)
items.explanation: 'Por mucho que' with imperfect subjunctive in past narrative. Futile search in story.

items.question: Ojalá _____ sido diferente, pero ya no se puede cambiar el pasado.
items.translation: (If only it had been different, but the past can no longer be changed)
items.explanation: 'Ojalá' with pluperfect subjunctive for impossible past wishes. Regret about unchangeable past.

items.question: Cualquiera que _____ sus razones, no justifica ese comportamiento inaceptable.
items.translation: (Whatever his reasons may be, it doesn't justify that unacceptable behavior)
items.explanation: 'Cualquiera que' (whatever) requires subjunctive. Unknown or irrelevant reasons.

items.question: Aunque _____ esa batalla crucial, no perdieron la guerra final.
items.translation: (Although they lost that crucial battle, they didn't lose the final war)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with indicative states known fact. When concession is fact, use indicative.

items.question: Posiblemente _____ llegado ya a su destino final si no se hubieran perdido.
items.translation: (They possibly would have already arrived at their final destination if they hadn't gotten lost)
items.explanation: 'Posiblemente' with conditional perfect for past possibility. Hypothetical past arrival.

items.question: Fuera lo que _____, nunca reveló su secreto más profundo a nadie.
items.translation: (Whatever it was, he never revealed his deepest secret to anyone)
items.explanation: 'Fuera lo que fuese/fuera' literary fixed expression. Alternative imperfect subjunctive form.

items.question: ¿_____? ¿Qué tal todo por tu barrio últimamente?
items.translation: (What's up? How's everything in your neighborhood lately?)
items.explanation: '¿Qué pasa?' very casual greeting. Street-level informal Spanish.

items.question: Estoy _____ de todo este asunto complicado que no termina nunca.
items.translation: (I'm totally fed up with all this complicated matter that never ends)
items.explanation: 'Hasta las narices' strong colloquial expression. Extreme frustration in informal speech.

items.question: Me _____ completamente cuando me dijeron que había ganado la lotería.
items.translation: (I was completely shocked when they told me I had won the lottery)
items.explanation: 'Quedarse flipado' very colloquial amazement. Youth slang for extreme surprise.

items.question: Ese examen estaba _____, no entendí casi nada de las preguntas.
items.translation: (That exam was super hard, I didn't understand almost any of the questions)
items.explanation: 'Estar tirado' colloquial for very difficult. Informal expression for extreme difficulty (ironic meaning).

items.question: ¿Te _____ si vamos al cine esta noche en vez de quedarnos?
items.translation: (Do you feel like going to the movies tonight instead of staying in?)
items.explanation: '¿Te apetece?' casual for 'feel like'. Common in conversational Spanish.

items.question: No me _____ ni un pelo de lo que dice ese político corrupto.
items.translation: (I don't believe even a bit of what that corrupt politician says)
items.explanation: 'No creer ni un pelo' colloquial total disbelief. Informal complete skepticism.

items.question: Está _____ que ganemos el partido después de jugar tan mal.
items.translation: (It's impossible we'll win the game after playing so badly)
items.explanation: 'Estar tirado' can mean impossible (colloquial). Informal way to say very unlikely.

items.question: Me da _____ tener que pedirle dinero prestado a mi padre otra vez.
items.translation: (It embarrasses me to have to borrow money from my father again)
items.explanation: 'Dar corte' colloquial embarrassment in Spain. Informal shame expression.

items.question: Ese tipo me cae _____, siempre está criticando a todo el mundo.
items.translation: (I can't stand that guy, he's always criticizing everyone)
items.explanation: 'Caer fatal' strong dislike. More intense than 'caer mal'.

items.question: ¡Qué _____ de fiesta! Me divertí muchísimo con todos mis amigos.
items.translation: (What an awesome party! I had so much fun with all my friends)
items.explanation: 'Qué pasada de' colloquial for amazing. Youth slang for excellent.

items.question: Esto está _____, no hay manera de resolverlo fácilmente.
items.translation: (This is screwed up, there's no way to solve it easily)
items.explanation: 'Estar chungo' colloquial for problematic. Informal expression for bad situation.

items.question: Me _____ mogollón tu nuevo coche deportivo tan elegante.
items.translation: (I really like your elegant new sports car a lot)
items.explanation: 'Molar' colloquial for liking. Youth slang equivalent of 'gustar'.

items.question: Ese restaurante está _____, tienen la mejor comida de toda la ciudad.
items.translation: (That restaurant is awesome, they have the best food in the entire city)
items.explanation: 'Estar genial' colloquial excellence. Informal high praise.

items.question: No tengo ni _____ de lo que está hablando ese profesor aburrido.
items.translation: (I don't have the slightest idea what that boring professor is talking about)
items.explanation: 'Ni pajolera idea' emphatic colloquial ignorance. Strong informal expression.

items.question: Me _____ mogollón cuando la gente llega tarde sin avisar antes.
items.translation: (It really bugs me when people arrive late without warning first)
items.explanation: 'Mosquear' colloquial for annoying. Informal irritation expression.

items.question: Cabe _____ que los resultados pueden variar según las circunstancias.
items.translation: (It should be noted that results may vary according to circumstances)
items.explanation: 'Cabe señalar' formal academic phrase. Scholarly writing uses 'cabe' for noting.

items.question: A _____ de esta investigación, se puede concluir lo siguiente.
items.translation: (Based on this research, the following can be concluded)
items.explanation: 'A raíz de' formal for 'based on'. Academic foundation statement.

items.question: _____ destacar la importancia fundamental de este descubrimiento científico.
items.translation: (It is worth highlighting the fundamental importance of this scientific discovery)
items.explanation: 'Cabe destacar' academic emphasis phrase. Scholarly highlighting of significance.

items.question: En _____ a los datos presentados, se observa una tendencia clara.
items.translation: (Regarding the data presented, a clear trend is observed)
items.explanation: 'En cuanto a' formal for 'regarding'. Academic transitional phrase.

items.question: Se _____ concluir que la hipótesis inicial era correcta después de todo.
items.translation: (It can be concluded that the initial hypothesis was correct after all)
items.explanation: 'Se puede concluir' formal conclusion statement. Academic result presentation.

items.question: _____ la evidencia presentada, resulta claro que se necesitan más estudios.
items.translation: (Given the evidence presented, it's clear more studies are needed)
items.explanation: 'Dada' (given) formal academic conditional. Scholarly reasoning based on evidence.

items.question: Los resultados _____ a demostrar la validez de nuestra teoría propuesta.
items.translation: (The results tend to demonstrate the validity of our proposed theory)
items.explanation: 'Tender a' academic for showing tendency. Scholarly analysis of patterns.

items.question: _____ resaltar que este método tiene limitaciones que deben considerarse.
items.translation: (It should be emphasized this method has limitations that must be considered)
items.explanation: 'Conviene resaltar' formal academic advisement. Scholarly cautionary statement.

items.question: Los hallazgos _____ en concordancia con estudios previos realizados.
items.translation: (The findings are in agreement with previous studies conducted)
items.explanation: 'Estar en concordancia' formal agreement phrase. Academic alignment statement.

items.question: Cabe _____ la posibilidad de que existan factores no considerados anteriormente.
items.translation: (The possibility exists that there are factors not previously considered)
items.explanation: 'Cabe contemplar' academic consideration phrase. Scholarly openness to alternatives.

items.question: En _____ de lo anterior, se propone una nueva metodología de análisis.
items.translation: (In view of the foregoing, a new analysis methodology is proposed)
items.explanation: 'En virtud de' formal for 'in view of'. Legal and academic reasoning.

items.question: Los datos _____ la necesidad de realizar investigaciones adicionales profundas.
items.translation: (The data suggest the need to conduct additional in-depth research)
items.explanation: 'Sugerir' academic for tentative conclusions. Scholarly caution about implications.

items.question: _____ mencionar que la muestra utilizada podría no ser representativa.
items.translation: (It's worth mentioning the sample used might not be representative)
items.explanation: 'Cabe mencionar' academic caveat phrase. Scholarly acknowledgment of limitations.

items.question: Se _____ por tanto que se requiere más investigación en este campo.
items.translation: (It follows therefore that more research is required in this field)
items.explanation: 'Se desprende' formal for 'it follows'. Academic logical conclusion.

items.question: A _____ de lo expuesto, podemos afirmar con certeza razonable que...
items.translation: (In light of what has been presented, we can state with reasonable certainty that...)
items.explanation: 'A tenor de' very formal for 'in light of'. High-level academic phrasing.

items.question: Me prometió que me _____ cuando tuviera noticias importantes del proyecto.
items.translation: (He promised me he would call me when he had important news about the project)
items.explanation: Future in direct speech becomes conditional in reported speech. Past reporting pulls future to conditional.

items.question: Ella dijo que ya _____ terminado su trabajo antes de salir de la oficina.
items.translation: (She said she had already finished her work before leaving the office)
items.explanation: Present perfect in direct speech becomes pluperfect in past reported speech. Time reference shifts backward.

items.question: Me preguntó si yo _____ ir con ellos al concierto del sábado próximo.
items.translation: (He asked me if I could go with them to next Saturday's concert)
items.explanation: Questions in reported speech use conditional for future possibility. 'Can you' becomes 'if I could'.

items.question: Nos informaron que el vuelo _____ retrasado por problemas técnicos.
items.translation: (They informed us the flight had been delayed due to technical problems)
items.explanation: Past event before reporting moment uses pluperfect. Prior completion relative to informing.

items.question: Comentó que nunca _____ a España pero que le encantaría visitarla.
items.translation: (He commented he had never been to Spain but would love to visit it)
items.explanation: Never having done something by reporting time uses pluperfect. Life experience up to comment.

items.question: El profesor nos advirtió que el examen final _____ mucho más difícil.
items.translation: (The professor warned us the final exam would be much more difficult)
items.explanation: Future from past perspective uses conditional. Warning about future event in reported speech.

items.question: Me confesó que _____ muy nervioso durante toda la entrevista de trabajo.
items.translation: (He confessed to me he had been very nervous throughout the entire job interview)
items.explanation: Completed past state before confessing uses pluperfect. Interview happened before confession.

items.question: Dijeron que nos _____ saber los resultados en cuanto los tuvieran listos.
items.translation: (They said they would let us know the results as soon as they had them ready)
items.explanation: Future promise from past uses conditional. 'Will let you know' becomes 'would let know'.

items.question: Aseguró que para entonces ya _____ todo el dinero que debía al banco.
items.translation: (He assured that by then he would have already paid all the money he owed to the bank)
items.explanation: Future perfect in direct speech becomes conditional perfect in reported. Future completion from past perspective.

items.question: Nos explicó que sus padres _____ viviendo en el extranjero desde hacía años.
items.translation: (He explained to us his parents had been living abroad for years)
items.explanation: Ongoing past duration uses imperfect in reported speech. Background state at time of explanation.

items.question: Me aconsejó que _____ más atención en clase para mejorar mis notas.
items.translation: (She advised me to pay more attention in class to improve my grades)
items.explanation: Commands/advice in reported speech become imperfect subjunctive. Recommendation from past uses past subjunctive.

items.question: Afirmó rotundamente que él no _____ nada que ver con el escándalo.
items.translation: (He categorically stated he had nothing to do with the scandal)
items.explanation: Present denial in direct becomes imperfect in reported. Ongoing non-involvement statement.

items.question: Me comentó que _____ estudiando español desde hacía tres años sin interrupción.
items.translation: (She told me she had been studying Spanish for three years without interruption)
items.explanation: Progressive duration statement uses imperfect in reported speech. Ongoing learning at comment time.

items.question: Declaró públicamente que nunca _____ a renunciar a sus principios fundamentales.
items.translation: (He publicly declared he would never give up his fundamental principles)
items.explanation: Future intention from past uses imperfect of 'ir a'. Determination expressed in past.

items.question: Mencionó que ese día _____ el más feliz de toda su vida hasta ahora.
items.translation: (He mentioned that that day had been the happiest of his entire life so far)
items.explanation: Completed past event before mentioning uses pluperfect. Day completed before the telling.

items.question: El edificio histórico _____ por el famoso arquitecto Gaudí en 1910.
items.translation: (The historic building was designed by the famous architect Gaudí in 1910)
items.explanation: 'Ser' passive for specific completed action with agent. Definite past event with known doer uses 'fue'.

items.question: _____ prohibido estrictamente fumar en todos los espacios cerrados del edificio.
items.translation: (Smoking is strictly prohibited in all enclosed spaces of the building)
items.explanation: 'Estar' + past participle for resulting state. Current prohibition state from past action.

items.question: La novela _____ traducida a más de cincuenta idiomas diferentes alrededor del mundo.
items.translation: (The novel has been translated into more than fifty different languages around the world)
items.explanation: Present perfect passive for completed action relevant now. Translation history affecting present status.

items.question: _____ vendieron todas las entradas para el espectáculo en menos de una hora.
items.translation: (All tickets for the show were sold in less than one hour)
items.explanation: Passive 'se' preferred over 'ser' passive in Spanish. More natural for indefinite agent.

items.question: Los documentos importantes _____ guardados bajo llave en la caja fuerte de seguridad.
items.translation: (The important documents are kept under lock and key in the safety deposit box)
items.explanation: 'Estar' + participle for current location state. Resulting condition of storage.

items.question: El proyecto _____ siendo supervisado personalmente por el director ejecutivo.
items.translation: (The project is being personally supervised by the executive director)
items.explanation: Progressive passive uses 'estar siendo' + participle. Ongoing passive action right now.

items.question: _____ construyendo un nuevo hospital moderno en las afueras de la ciudad.
items.translation: (A new modern hospital is being built on the outskirts of the city)
items.explanation: Progressive passive 'se' for ongoing construction. Current building process without agent.

items.question: La teoría _____ formulada por el científico hace más de un siglo.
items.translation: (The theory was formulated by the scientist over a century ago)
items.explanation: 'Ser' passive with specific agent in definite past. Historical formulation with known scientist.

items.question: _____ aceptan tarjetas de crédito en la mayoría de los comercios modernos.
items.translation: (Credit cards are accepted in most modern businesses)
items.explanation: General passive 'se' for universal practice. Common business policy statement.

items.question: El acuerdo comercial _____ firmado después de meses de negociaciones intensas.
items.translation: (The commercial agreement was signed after months of intense negotiations)
items.explanation: 'Ser' passive for completed formal event. Specific signing after negotiation process.

items.question: _____ cerradas todas las ventanas antes de que empezara la tormenta fuerte.
items.translation: (All windows were closed before the strong storm started)
items.explanation: 'Ser' passive for completed action. Specific closing action before storm.

items.question: La ceremonia _____ celebrada en el salón principal del palacio presidencial.
items.translation: (The ceremony was celebrated in the main hall of the presidential palace)
items.explanation: 'Ser' passive for formal ceremonial event. Specific celebration with location.

items.question: _____ necesita personal con experiencia para el nuevo departamento de ventas.
items.translation: (Experienced personnel is needed for the new sales department)
items.explanation: Impersonal 'se' for hiring needs. Job requirement statement without agent.

items.question: Los resultados _____ anunciados mañana durante la conferencia de prensa oficial.
items.translation: (The results will be announced tomorrow during the official press conference)
items.explanation: Future 'ser' passive for planned formal announcement. Scheduled revelation of results.

items.question: La decisión final _____ tomada por el comité directivo en reunión extraordinaria.
items.translation: (The final decision was made by the board of directors in extraordinary meeting)
items.explanation: 'Ser' passive with specific agent for important decision. Formal resolution by identified body.

items.question: Te avisaré en cuanto _____ alguna novedad importante sobre el asunto.
items.translation: (I'll let you know as soon as I have any important news about the matter)
items.explanation: 'En cuanto' with future reference requires subjunctive. Waiting for uncertain future information.

items.question: Vamos a esperar aquí hasta que _____ nuestro amigo que viene en tren.
items.translation: (We're going to wait here until our friend who's coming by train arrives)
items.explanation: 'Hasta que' with pending action uses subjunctive. Future-oriented waiting requires this mood.

items.question: Cuando _____ más experiencia laboral, podrás solicitar mejores puestos de trabajo.
items.translation: (When you have more work experience, you'll be able to apply for better job positions)
items.explanation: Future time clause with 'cuando' requires subjunctive. Hypothetical future moment needs subjunctive.

items.question: Mientras _____ en esta casa, tendrás que respetar todas nuestras reglas.
items.translation: (While you live in this house, you'll have to respect all our rules)
items.explanation: 'Mientras' with future or indefinite duration uses subjunctive. Ongoing future condition.

items.question: Tan pronto como _____ del aeropuerto, iré directamente a verte a tu casa.
items.translation: (As soon as I arrive from the airport, I'll go directly to see you at your house)
items.explanation: 'Tan pronto como' with future triggers subjunctive. Immediate future action upon condition.

items.question: Antes de que _____ demasiado tarde, deberías disculparte sinceramente con ella.
items.translation: (Before it's too late, you should apologize sincerely to her)
items.explanation: 'Antes de que' always requires subjunctive. Prevention of future undesirable state.

items.question: Después de que _____ de llover afuera, saldremos a dar un paseo por el parque.
items.translation: (After it stops raining outside, we'll go out for a walk through the park)
items.explanation: 'Después de que' with future event requires subjunctive. Planning for uncertain future cessation.

items.question: No podré relajarme hasta que no _____ terminado completamente este informe urgente.
items.translation: (I won't be able to relax until I've completely finished this urgent report)
items.explanation: 'Hasta que' with completion requires present perfect subjunctive. Future completion blocking relaxation.

items.question: Una vez que _____ tu decisión final, no hay vuelta atrás en este proceso.
items.translation: (Once you make your final decision, there's no turning back in this process)
items.explanation: 'Una vez que' with future consequence uses subjunctive. Irreversible future choice.

items.question: Cada vez que _____ a ese restaurante, siempre pido el mismo plato delicioso.
items.translation: (Every time I go to that restaurant, I always order the same delicious dish)
items.explanation: 'Cada vez que' with habitual action uses indicative. Repeated pattern uses indicative, not subjunctive.

items.question: Cuando _____ pequeño, siempre jugaba en el parque con mis amigos del barrio.
items.translation: (When I was little, I always used to play in the park with my neighborhood friends)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' referring to completed habitual past uses imperfect indicative. Childhood state is factual past.

items.question: Apenas _____ el examen final, me iré de vacaciones bien merecidas a la playa.
items.translation: (As soon as I finish the final exam, I'll go on well-deserved vacation to the beach)
items.explanation: 'Apenas' with future event requires subjunctive. Immediate action upon future completion.

items.question: Cuando _____ la lotería el mes pasado, no pude creerlo al principio.
items.translation: (When I won the lottery last month, I couldn't believe it at first)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with completed specific past uses preterite indicative. Definite past event uses indicative.

items.question: Siempre que _____ ayuda con algo, no dudes en llamarme cuando quieras.
items.translation: (Whenever you need help with something, don't hesitate to call me whenever you want)
items.explanation: 'Siempre que' meaning 'whenever' with future uses subjunctive. Open-ended future occasions.

items.question: No saldremos de casa hasta que no _____ de llover completamente afuera.
items.translation: (We won't leave the house until it completely stops raining outside)
items.explanation: 'Hasta que' with completion uses present perfect subjunctive. Waiting for finished rain cessation.

name: Register and Formality
description: Navigate formal vs informal language contexts

items.question: ¿_____ usted ayudarme con este problema técnico tan complicado, por favor?
items.translation: (Could you help me with this very complicated technical problem, please?)
items.explanation: Formal polite request uses conditional of 'poder' with usted. Professional courtesy requires conditional.

items.question: Le _____ mucho si pudiera responder a mi solicitud lo antes posible.
items.translation: (I would appreciate it very much if you could respond to my request as soon as possible)
items.explanation: Formal gratitude uses conditional for politeness. Business correspondence requires conditional.

items.question: _____, ¿me pasas la sal que está al otro lado de la mesa?
items.translation: (Hey, can you pass me the salt that's on the other side of the table?)
items.explanation: Informal 'oye' for casual attention-getting. Friends and family use informal forms.

items.question: _____, ¿podría indicarme dónde se encuentra la oficina del director general?
items.translation: (Excuse me, could you tell me where the general manager's office is located?)
items.explanation: Formal 'disculpe' for polite address to strangers. Professional environment requires formality.

items.question: Querido amigo, ¿_____ venir a mi fiesta de cumpleaños este sábado?
items.translation: (Dear friend, can you come to my birthday party this Saturday?)
items.explanation: Informal direct question to friend uses present tense. Close relationships use simple forms.

items.question: Estimado señor Director, le escribo para _____ mi candidatura al puesto.
items.translation: (Dear Director, I'm writing to present my application for the position)
items.explanation: Formal letter uses infinitive after 'para'. 'Le' already shows formal indirect object.

items.question: ¿Te _____ si te hago una pregunta muy personal sobre tu familia?
items.translation: (Do you mind if I ask you a very personal question about your family?)
items.explanation: Informal direct question uses present. Close relationship allows simple present.

items.question: Le _____ que tome asiento mientras espera su turno para la consulta.
items.translation: (I ask you to please take a seat while you wait your turn for the consultation)
items.explanation: 'Rogar' most polite formal request. Medical/professional settings use highest formality.

items.question: Tío, ¿_____ prestarme tu coche para ir a la fiesta esta noche?
items.translation: (Dude, can you lend me your car to go to the party tonight?)
items.explanation: Very informal 'tío' requires matching informal verb. Casual request between friends.

items.question: Señoría, _____ permiso para dirigirme al tribunal con mi testimonio.
items.translation: (Your Honor, I request permission to address the court with my testimony)
items.explanation: 'Solicitar' highest formality for legal contexts. Court proceedings require utmost formal register.

items.question: ¿_____ acompañarme a la tienda? No quiero ir solo en este momento.
items.translation: (Would you accompany me to the store? I don't want to go alone right now)
items.explanation: Informal friendly request uses present. Direct question to friend uses simple tense.

items.question: Quisiera _____ una consulta médica para la próxima semana si es posible.
items.translation: (I would like to schedule a medical appointment for next week if possible)
items.explanation: 'Quisiera solicitar' very formal medical request. Healthcare settings require formal register.

items.question: Oiga, _____, ¿este autobús va hasta el centro comercial de la ciudad?
items.translation: (Excuse me, please, does this bus go to the city shopping center?)
items.explanation: Full 'por favor' maintains formal tone with 'oiga'. Stranger address requires standard politeness.

items.question: Me _____ en comunicarle que su solicitud ha sido aprobada favorablemente.
items.translation: (I am pleased to inform you that your application has been favorably approved)
items.explanation: 'Me complazco en' ultra-formal business announcement. Official communication requires highest formality.

items.question: Tío, ¿qué _____, vamos al cine o nos quedamos viendo películas en casa?
items.translation: (Dude, what do you say, shall we go to the movies or stay watching films at home?)
items.explanation: Casual 'tío' demands informal present tense. Very informal proposal between close friends.

items.question: Este restaurante es _____ el que fuimos la semana pasada al centro.
items.translation: (This restaurant is much better than the one we went to downtown last week)
items.explanation: 'Mejor' is irregular comparative. Intensify with 'mucho', never 'más mejor' or 'muy mejor'.

items.question: Mi hermana es dos años _____ yo, nació en el mismo mes de julio.
items.translation: (My sister is two years younger than me, she was born in the same month of July)
items.explanation: 'Menor' for age comparison, especially with numbers. More natural than 'más joven' with specific years.

items.question: Esta película es _____ todas las que he visto este año en el cine.
items.translation: (This movie is the most interesting of all I've seen this year at the cinema)
items.explanation: Superlative requires article + más/menos + adjective + 'de'. Within group uses 'de'.

items.question: Trabajo _____ horas _____ pensaba cuando acepté este puesto demandante.
items.translation: (I work many more hours than I thought when I accepted this demanding position)
items.explanation: Comparative with countable nouns uses 'muchas más'. 'De las que' for clause comparison.

items.question: Cuanto _____ lo pienso detenidamente, menos seguro estoy de mi decisión inicial.
items.translation: (The more I think about it carefully, the less sure I am of my initial decision)
items.explanation: 'Cuanto más...menos' expresses proportional relationship. Increasing thought decreases certainty.

items.question: Este problema es _____ complicado _____ parece a simple vista superficial.
items.translation: (This problem is more complicated than it appears at superficial first glance)
items.explanation: 'Más...de lo que' for comparing with clause. Abstract comparison uses 'de lo que'.

items.question: Ella es _____ inteligente _____ su hermana gemela que estudia medicina.
items.translation: (She is as intelligent as her twin sister who studies medicine)
items.explanation: 'Tan...como' for equal comparison of qualities. Adjectives use 'tan', not 'tanto'.

items.question: Tengo _____ trabajo _____ tú en este momento del proyecto importante.
items.translation: (I have as much work as you at this moment of the important project)
items.explanation: 'Tanto...como' for equal amounts. Uncountable 'trabajo' uses 'tanto'.

items.question: Es el _____ día de toda mi vida profesional hasta este momento.
items.translation: (It's the best day of my entire professional life up to this moment)
items.explanation: Superlative of 'bueno' is irregular 'mejor'. Never use 'más bueno' for superlative.

items.question: Tiene _____ experiencia _____ cualquier otro candidato que se presentó al puesto.
items.translation: (He has more experience than any other candidate who applied for the position)
items.explanation: Direct comparison uses 'más...que'. Comparing to another person uses 'que'.

items.question: Este hotel es _____ caro _____ pensábamos después de ver los precios online.
items.translation: (This hotel is less expensive than we thought after seeing the prices online)
items.explanation: 'Menos...de lo que' for comparison with expectation. Comparing to thought uses 'de lo que'.

items.question: Mi hermano _____ es médico cirujano especializado en traumatología avanzada.
items.translation: (My older brother is a specialized surgeon in advanced traumatology)
items.explanation: 'Mayor' for older sibling. Age within family uses irregular comparative.

items.question: Prefiero quedarme en casa _____ salir con este tiempo tan horrible afuera.
items.translation: (I prefer staying home rather than going out in this horrible weather outside)
items.explanation: 'Preferir...antes que' for strong preference. Choosing one over another emphatically.

items.question: La situación económica actual es _____ peor _____ la que vivimos hace diez años.
items.translation: (The current economic situation is much worse than what we experienced ten years ago)
items.explanation: 'Peor' is irregular comparative. Intensify with 'mucho', never 'más peor' or 'muy peor'.

items.question: Gastamos _____ dinero _____ habíamos presupuestado originalmente para el viaje.
items.translation: (We spent more money than we had originally budgeted for the trip)
items.explanation: 'Más...del que' when comparing quantities with specific noun. 'Dinero' is specified, use 'del que'.

items.question: No voy a _____ por las ramas, te diré directamente lo que pienso.
items.translation: (I'm not going to beat around the bush, I'll tell you directly what I think)
items.explanation: 'Andar por las ramas' means beat around the bush. Avoiding direct communication idiom.

items.question: Después de discutir por horas, finalmente _____ en un punto medio aceptable.
items.translation: (After arguing for hours, we finally met halfway at an acceptable middle ground)
items.explanation: 'Ponerse de acuerdo' means reach agreement. Mutual decision-making process.

items.question: Tiene que _____ los pies en la tierra y dejar de soñar despierto.
items.translation: (He has to keep his feet on the ground and stop daydreaming)
items.explanation: 'Poner los pies en la tierra' means be realistic. Stop fantasizing and face reality.

items.question: Me _____ la lengua para no decir lo que realmente pensaba en ese momento.
items.translation: (I bit my tongue to not say what I really thought at that moment)
items.explanation: 'Morderse la lengua' means bite your tongue. Self-censorship to avoid conflict.

items.question: No le _____ vueltas al asunto y toma una decisión de una vez por todas.
items.translation: (Don't keep going over the matter and make a decision once and for all)
items.explanation: 'Dar vueltas' means overthink/go in circles. Excessive deliberation without action.

items.question: Me _____ la gota que colmó el vaso cuando llegó tarde otra vez.
items.translation: (That was the straw that broke the camel's back when he arrived late again)
items.explanation: 'La gota que colmó el vaso' means last straw. Final incident triggering reaction.

items.question: Estoy entre la espada y _____ con esta decisión tan difícil que debo tomar.
items.translation: (I'm between a rock and a hard place with this very difficult decision I must make)
items.explanation: 'Entre la espada y la pared' means between rock and hard place. Impossible dilemma.

items.question: Voy a _____ el toro por los cuernos y enfrentar el problema directamente.
items.translation: (I'm going to take the bull by the horns and face the problem directly)
items.explanation: 'Coger el toro por los cuernos' means tackle problem head-on. Confronting challenges directly.

items.question: Le _____ cuatro verdades muy claras y se quedó completamente callado.
items.translation: (I told him some hard truths very clearly and he was left completely silent)
items.explanation: 'Decir cuatro verdades' means tell hard truths. Speaking frankly about uncomfortable facts.

items.question: No voy a _____ paños calientes a la situación, es más grave de lo que parece.
items.translation: (I'm not going to sugarcoat the situation, it's more serious than it appears)
items.explanation: 'Poner paños calientes' means sugarcoat/downplay. Avoiding harsh reality with mild language.

items.question: Me _____ los pelos de punta cuando escuché esos ruidos extraños en la noche.
items.translation: (My hair stood on end when I heard those strange noises at night)
items.explanation: 'Poner los pelos de punta' means give goosebumps/terrify. Extreme fear reaction.

items.question: Ese comentario me _____ la mosca detrás de la oreja sobre sus verdaderas intenciones.
items.translation: (That comment put the fly behind my ear about his true intentions)
items.explanation: 'Poner la mosca detrás de la oreja' means raise suspicion. Creating doubt about trustworthiness.

items.question: Tienes que _____ el hombro y ayudarme con este proyecto tan importante.
items.translation: (You have to put your shoulder to the wheel and help me with this very important project)
items.explanation: 'Arrimar el hombro' means pitch in/help seriously. Contributing effort to collective task.

items.question: Siempre _____ las cosas por su nombre sin andarse con rodeos innecesarios.
items.translation: (He always calls things by their name without beating around the bush unnecessarily)
items.explanation: 'Llamar las cosas por su nombre' means call spade a spade. Direct honest communication.

items.question: Me _____ la mano con ese negocio que parecía tan prometedor al principio.
items.translation: (That business that seemed so promising at first burned my hand)
items.explanation: 'Quemar la mano' means get burned in deal. Bad investment or betrayed trust.

items.question: Espero sinceramente que _____ aprobar todos tus exámenes finales este semestre.
items.translation: (I sincerely hope you manage to pass all your final exams this semester)
items.explanation: 'Esperar que' triggers subjunctive for hopes. Wishing for someone's success requires subjunctive.

items.question: Ojalá que mañana _____ buen tiempo para poder ir a la playa finalmente.
items.translation: (I hope tomorrow has good weather so we can finally go to the beach)
items.explanation: 'Ojalá que' always uses subjunctive. Wishing for future weather condition.

items.question: Quiero que mis hijos _____ felices y exitosos en todo lo que emprendan.
items.translation: (I want my children to be happy and successful in everything they undertake)
items.explanation: 'Querer que' triggers subjunctive for desires. Wanting something for others requires subjunctive.

items.question: Deseo profundamente que _____ encontrar la solución que buscas tan arduamente.
items.translation: (I deeply wish you find the solution you're searching for so arduously)
items.explanation: 'Desear que' expresses strong wish with subjunctive. Hoping for capability requires this mood.

items.question: Prefiero que tú _____ la decisión final porque conoces mejor la situación.
items.translation: (I prefer that you make the final decision because you know the situation better)
items.explanation: 'Preferir que' shows preference requiring subjunctive. Wanting someone else to decide.

items.question: Necesito urgentemente que alguien me _____ con esta tarea tan complicada.
items.translation: (I urgently need someone to help me with this very complicated task)
items.explanation: 'Necesitar que' expresses need with subjunctive. Requiring someone's assistance.

items.question: Me gustaría muchísimo que tú _____ venir a visitarme pronto a mi nueva casa.
items.translation: (I would very much like you to come visit me soon at my new house)
items.explanation: 'Me gustaría que' uses imperfect subjunctive for polite wish. Conditional main verb pulls imperfect subjunctive.

items.question: Espero que no _____ ningún problema grave durante tu viaje al extranjero.
items.translation: (I hope you don't have any serious problems during your trip abroad)
items.explanation: Hoping against problems uses negative subjunctive. Wishing for absence of difficulty.

items.question: Quiero que _____ la verdad antes de que sea demasiado tarde para actuar.
items.translation: (I want you to know the truth before it's too late to act)
items.explanation: 'Querer que' with knowledge verb uses subjunctive. Desiring someone's awareness.

items.question: Ojalá _____ podido asistir a tu boda, pero estaba fuera del país entonces.
items.translation: (I wish I could have attended your wedding, but I was out of the country then)
items.explanation: 'Ojalá' with pluperfect subjunctive for impossible past wish. Regret about missed event.

items.question: Desearía que las cosas _____ sido diferentes en aquella época difícil.
items.translation: (I wish things had been different in that difficult time)
items.explanation: Conditional 'desearía' with pluperfect subjunctive for past contrary-to-fact. Impossible past wish.

items.question: Espero que este mensaje te _____ bien y con buena salud en estos momentos.
items.translation: (I hope this message finds you well and in good health at this time)
items.explanation: Letter formula 'espero que te encuentre' uses subjunctive. Formal correspondence wish.

items.question: Quisiera que me _____ más detalles específicos sobre el proyecto propuesto.
items.translation: (I would like you to give me more specific details about the proposed project)
items.explanation: 'Quisiera que' polite request uses imperfect subjunctive. Conditional wish pulls past subjunctive.

items.question: Ojalá que todos _____ en paz y armonía en este mundo tan conflictivo.
items.translation: (If only everyone could live in peace and harmony in this very conflictive world)
items.explanation: 'Ojalá que' with imperfect subjunctive for unlikely present wish. Idealistic hope about current world.

items.question: Deseo fervientemente que _____ todos tus sueños más profundos y sinceros.
items.translation: (I fervently wish all your deepest and most sincere dreams come true)
items.explanation: 'Desear que' with subjunctive for heartfelt wishes. Dream fulfillment requires subjunctive.

name: Subjunctive - Indefinite Antecedent
description: Adjective clauses with unknown or nonexistent referents

items.question: Busco un apartamento que _____ cerca del metro y que no sea demasiado caro.
items.translation: (I'm looking for an apartment that's close to the metro and that's not too expensive)
items.explanation: Unknown apartment requires subjunctive. Seeking something that may or may not exist.

items.question: Necesitamos contratar a alguien que _____ hablar perfectamente tres idiomas europeos.
items.translation: (We need to hire someone who can speak three European languages perfectly)
items.explanation: Indefinite person with required skill uses subjunctive. Searching for qualified but unknown candidate.

items.question: ¿Hay alguien aquí que _____ arreglar computadoras con problemas de hardware?
items.translation: (Is there anyone here who knows how to fix computers with hardware problems?)
items.explanation: Question about possible person uses subjunctive. Unknown if such person exists.

items.question: No conozco a nadie que _____ trabajar tan duro como ella sin quejarse nunca.
items.translation: (I don't know anyone who works as hard as she does without ever complaining)
