items.explanation: Negative antecedent 'nadie' requires subjunctive. Denying existence of such person.

items.question: Queremos vivir en un lugar donde _____ tranquilidad y belleza natural incomparable.
items.translation: (We want to live in a place where there's tranquility and incomparable natural beauty)
items.explanation: Indefinite ideal place uses subjunctive. Seeking location that may be idealized.

items.question: No hay ningún restaurante en esta zona que _____ comida vegetariana orgánica.
items.translation: (There's no restaurant in this area that serves organic vegetarian food)
items.explanation: Negative existence 'ningún' requires subjunctive. Denying any restaurant with feature.

items.question: Buscamos una solución innovadora que _____ resolver todos estos problemas complejos.
items.translation: (We're looking for an innovative solution that can solve all these complex problems)
items.explanation: Indefinite solution being sought uses subjunctive. Unknown if such solution exists.

items.question: ¿Conoces algún libro que _____ sobre la historia medieval de España en detalle?
items.translation: (Do you know any book that talks about medieval Spanish history in detail?)
items.explanation: Question about possible book uses subjunctive. Uncertain if such book is known.

items.question: No encuentro a nadie que me _____ entender realmente en momentos difíciles.
items.translation: (I can't find anyone who really understands me in difficult moments)
items.explanation: Negative finding with 'nadie' requires subjunctive. Lamenting absence of understanding person.

items.question: Necesito un coche que _____ espacio suficiente para toda mi familia numerosa.
items.translation: (I need a car that has enough space for my entire large family)
items.explanation: Seeking unidentified car with feature uses subjunctive. Need for vehicle meeting criteria.

items.question: Queremos contratar empleados que _____ comprometidos con los valores de nuestra empresa.
items.translation: (We want to hire employees who are committed to our company's values)
items.explanation: Desired but unidentified employees use subjunctive. Seeking people with specific qualities.

items.question: Buscan un candidato que _____ experiencia previa en gestión de proyectos internacionales.
items.translation: (They're looking for a candidate who has previous experience in international project management)
items.explanation: Job requirement for unknown candidate uses subjunctive. Qualification search for indefinite person.

items.question: No hay nada que me _____ más feliz que pasar tiempo con mi familia.
items.translation: (There's nothing that makes me happier than spending time with my family)
items.explanation: Superlative with 'nada que' requires subjunctive. Extreme comparison with negative.

items.question: ¿Hay alguna farmacia por aquí que _____ abierta las veinticuatro horas del día?
items.translation: (Is there any pharmacy around here that's open twenty-four hours a day?)
items.explanation: Question about possible business uses subjunctive. Uncertain if 24-hour pharmacy exists nearby.

items.question: Quiero comprar una casa que _____ jardín grande y piscina para los niños.
items.translation: (I want to buy a house that has a large garden and pool for the children)
items.explanation: Desired but unidentified house uses subjunctive. Dream home requirements require subjunctive.

items.question: _____ más cuidado con tus cosas personales cuando viajes al extranjero.
items.translation: (Have more care with your personal belongings when you travel abroad - informal)
items.explanation: Irregular tú command of 'tener' is 'ten'. Drops -er and adds -en becomes 'ten'.

items.question: _____ paciente, todo llegará a su debido tiempo en la vida.
items.translation: (Be patient, everything will come in its due time in life - informal)
items.explanation: Irregular tú command of 'ser' is 'sé'. Very irregular, same as 'yo sé' from saber.

items.question: _____ a la tienda y compra leche, pan y huevos frescos para el desayuno.
items.translation: (Go to the store and buy milk, bread, and fresh eggs for breakfast - informal)
items.explanation: Irregular tú command of 'ir' is 've'. Extremely irregular command form.

items.question: No _____ miedo de intentar cosas nuevas y desafiantes en tu vida.
items.translation: (Don't be afraid to try new and challenging things in your life - informal)
items.explanation: Negative tú command uses present subjunctive. 'No tengas' for don't have/be.

items.question: _____ lo que te digo y sígueme sin hacer preguntas ahora mismo.
items.translation: (Do what I tell you and follow me without asking questions right now - informal)
items.explanation: Irregular tú command of 'hacer' is 'haz'. Drops -cer ending irregularly.

items.question: _____ responsable de tus propias acciones y decisiones importantes en la vida.
items.translation: (Be responsible for your own actions and important decisions in life - informal)
items.explanation: Irregular 'ser' command for character trait. 'Sé responsable' is standard advice.

items.question: _____ usted la amabilidad de esperar un momento aquí en la sala, por favor.
items.translation: (Please be so kind as to wait a moment here in the waiting room - formal)
items.explanation: Formal usted command of 'tener' is 'tenga'. Professional courtesy requires formal command.

items.question: No _____ eso nunca más, fue una falta de respeto grave e inaceptable.
items.translation: (Don't do that ever again, it was a serious and unacceptable lack of respect - informal)
items.explanation: Negative tú command uses subjunctive. 'No hagas' for prohibition.

items.question: _____ con nosotros al evento especial que organizamos para recaudar fondos.
items.translation: (Come with us to the special event we're organizing to raise funds - informal)
items.explanation: Irregular tú command of 'venir' is 'ven'. Invitation to accompany.

items.question: _____ la verdad completa, aunque sea difícil de escuchar para todos.
items.translation: (Tell the truth, even if it's difficult for everyone to hear - informal)
items.explanation: Irregular tú command of 'decir' is 'di'. Very short irregular form.

items.question: _____ constante en tu esfuerzo y verás resultados positivos muy pronto.
items.translation: (Be constant in your effort and you'll see positive results very soon - informal)
items.explanation: 'Ser' command for character quality. 'Sé constante' encourages perseverance.

items.question: _____ usted la bondad de firmar estos documentos oficiales aquí abajo.
items.translation: (Please be so kind as to sign these official documents down here - formal)
items.explanation: Formal request with 'tener la bondad'. Polite business transaction language.

items.question: No _____ tan impaciente, las cosas buenas requieren tiempo y paciencia.
items.translation: (Don't be so impatient, good things require time and patience - informal)
items.explanation: Negative command of 'ser' uses subjunctive. 'No seas' for character correction.

items.question: _____ amable con todas las personas que conozcas en tu camino de vida.
items.translation: (Be kind to all people you meet on your life path - informal)
items.explanation: 'Ser' command for moral instruction. 'Sé amable' is common life advice.

items.question: _____ precaución al conducir en estas condiciones climáticas tan peligrosas.
items.translation: (Have caution when driving in these very dangerous weather conditions - informal)
items.explanation: 'Tener' command for safety advice. 'Ten precaución' warns of danger.

name: Complex Conditional Sentences
description: Si clauses with mixed tenses and moods

items.question: Si _____ más tiempo libre disponible, viajaría por todo el mundo sin parar.
items.translation: (If I had more free time available, I would travel all over the world non-stop)
items.explanation: Hypothetical present uses imperfect subjunctive + conditional. Contrary to current reality.

items.question: Si hubieras estudiado más para el examen, lo _____ aprobado fácilmente ayer.
items.translation: (If you had studied more for the exam, you would have passed it easily yesterday)
items.explanation: Past contrary-to-fact uses pluperfect subjunctive + conditional perfect. Unrealized past possibility.

items.question: Si mañana _____ buen tiempo, iremos a la playa a disfrutar del sol.
items.translation: (If tomorrow has good weather, we'll go to the beach to enjoy the sun)
items.explanation: Real possible future condition uses present indicative. Likely future weather uses indicative.

items.question: Si yo _____ tú en esta situación complicada, renunciaría inmediatamente al trabajo.
items.translation: (If I were you in this complicated situation, I would resign immediately from the job)
items.explanation: 'Si yo fuera tú' standard advice formula. Hypothetical identity swap uses imperfect subjunctive.

items.question: Si no _____ lloviendo ahora mismo, saldríamos a caminar por el vecindario.
items.translation: (If it weren't raining right now, we would go out to walk around the neighborhood)
items.explanation: Contrary to present fact uses imperfect subjunctive. It IS raining, so hypothetical uses subjunctive.

items.question: Si _____ ganado la lotería el año pasado, ahora estaría viviendo en una mansión.
items.translation: (If I had won the lottery last year, I would now be living in a mansion)
items.explanation: Past condition with present result mixes pluperfect subjunctive + present conditional. Past hypothetical affecting now.

items.question: Si tú _____ conmigo mañana al médico, me sentiri mucho más tranquilo.
items.translation: (If you come with me tomorrow to the doctor, I'll feel much calmer)
items.explanation: Real likely future uses present indicative. Probable accompaniment doesn't need subjunctive.

items.question: Si _____ podido elegir libremente, habría estudiado arquitectura en la universidad.
items.translation: (If I had been able to choose freely, I would have studied architecture at university)
items.explanation: Past impossible condition uses pluperfect subjunctive + conditional perfect. Unrealized past choice.

items.question: Si no fuera por tu ayuda valiosa, nunca _____ completado este proyecto a tiempo.
items.translation: (If it weren't for your valuable help, I would never have completed this project on time)
items.explanation: 'Si no fuera por' with past result uses conditional perfect. Acknowledging crucial assistance.

items.question: Si me lo _____ pedido amablemente, te habría ayudado con mucho gusto.
items.translation: (If you had asked me kindly, I would have helped you very gladly)
items.explanation: Past hypothetical request uses pluperfect subjunctive. Different past approach would have changed outcome.

items.question: Si _____ rico y famoso, no cambiaría mi forma de ser ni mis valores.
items.translation: (If I were rich and famous, I wouldn't change my way of being or my values)
items.explanation: Hypothetical present state uses imperfect subjunctive. Contrary to current reality.

items.question: Si _____ más cuidadoso con el dinero, ahora no tendrías problemas financieros.
items.translation: (If you had been more careful with money, you wouldn't have financial problems now)
items.explanation: Past condition affecting present uses pluperfect subjunctive + present conditional. Past habits impact now.

items.question: Si el tren _____ a tiempo esta mañana, llegaremos perfectamente a la reunión.
items.translation: (If the train arrives on time this morning, we'll arrive perfectly to the meeting)
items.explanation: Real likely condition uses present indicative. Expected arrival uses indicative.

items.question: Si no _____ sido por aquel accidente grave, mi vida sería completamente diferente ahora.
items.translation: (If it hadn't been for that serious accident, my life would be completely different now)
items.explanation: Past event affecting present uses pluperfect subjunctive + conditional. Life-changing past event.

items.question: Si _____ la oportunidad de vivir en otro país, ¿dónde te gustaría estar?
items.translation: (If you had the opportunity to live in another country, where would you like to be?)
items.explanation: Hypothetical opportunity uses imperfect subjunctive. Imagining unlikely scenario.

items.question: Busco el libro que _____ sobre la Segunda Guerra Mundial en Europa.
items.translation: (I'm looking for the book that talks about World War II in Europe - I know it exists)
items.explanation: Indicative for known specific book. Speaker knows which book, so uses indicative.

items.question: Busco un libro que _____ sobre la Segunda Guerra Mundial con nuevas perspectivas.
items.translation: (I'm looking for a book that talks about World War II with new perspectives - don't know if it exists)
items.explanation: Subjunctive for indefinite unknown book. Not sure if such book exists.

items.question: Aunque _____ mucho dinero ahorrado, nunca gasta en cosas innecesarias o frívolas.
items.translation: (Although he has a lot of money saved, he never spends on unnecessary or frivolous things - FACT)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with indicative states known fact. Speaker affirms he does have money.

items.question: Aunque _____ mucho dinero disponible, no podría comprar la felicidad verdadera.
items.translation: (Even if I had a lot of money available, I couldn't buy true happiness - HYPOTHETICAL)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with subjunctive for hypothetical. Speaker doesn't have money, imagining if they did.

items.question: Cuando _____ niño pequeño, siempre jugaba con mis primos en el campo.
items.translation: (When I was a little child, I always used to play with my cousins in the countryside - PAST FACT)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' referring to factual past uses imperfect indicative. Childhood is known fact.

items.question: Cuando _____ grande y adulto, quiero ser médico como mi padre es ahora.
items.translation: (When I'm big and grown up, I want to be a doctor like my father is now - FUTURE)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with future reference requires subjunctive. Uncertain future time uses subjunctive.

items.question: Como _____ tarde al trabajo otra vez, el jefe se enfadará muchísimo contigo.
items.translation: (If you arrive late to work again, the boss will get very angry with you - WARNING)
items.explanation: 'Como' meaning 'if' in warning uses subjunctive. Conditional warning about future.

items.question: Como _____ tarde a la reunión importante, perdí información clave del proyecto.
items.translation: (Since I arrived late to the important meeting, I missed key project information - FACT)
items.explanation: 'Como' meaning 'since/because' uses indicative. Explaining factual past cause.

items.question: Creo firmemente que él _____ la capacidad de resolver este problema complejo.
items.translation: (I firmly believe he has the capacity to solve this complex problem - BELIEF)
items.explanation: Affirmative 'creer que' uses indicative. Stating belief as fact requires indicative.

items.question: No creo en absoluto que él _____ la capacidad necesaria para este puesto.
items.translation: (I don't believe at all he has the necessary capacity for this position - DOUBT)
items.explanation: Negative 'no creer que' triggers subjunctive. Doubt about capacity requires subjunctive.

items.question: Tal vez _____ razón en lo que dices sobre la situación económica actual.
items.translation: (Perhaps you're right in what you say about the current economic situation - UNCERTAINTY)
items.explanation: 'Tal vez' with subjunctive emphasizes doubt. Uncertain agreement uses subjunctive.

items.question: Tal vez _____ razón ayer cuando me advertiste sobre ese peligro evidente.
items.translation: (Perhaps you were right yesterday when you warned me about that evident danger - PAST FACT)
items.explanation: 'Tal vez' can use indicative for past facts. Past agreement uses imperfect indicative.

items.question: Donde tú _____, yo también iré para apoyarte en todo momento difícil.
items.translation: (Wherever you go, I'll also go to support you in every difficult moment - FUTURE)
items.explanation: 'Donde' with future reference requires subjunctive. Unknown future locations need subjunctive.

items.question: Donde él _____ ayer, encontró exactamente lo que estaba buscando desde hace tiempo.
items.translation: (Where he went yesterday, he found exactly what he had been looking for - PAST FACT)
items.explanation: 'Donde' with known past fact uses preterite indicative. Definite past location.

items.question: Mientras tú _____ aquí conmigo, no te faltará nada que necesites para vivir.
items.translation: (While you're here with me, you won't lack anything you need to live - FUTURE)
items.explanation: 'Mientras' with future or indefinite time uses subjunctive. Open-ended future duration.

items.question: _____, me gustaría comentar algunos puntos importantes sobre la propuesta presentada.
items.translation: (First of all, I would like to comment on some important points about the presented proposal)
items.explanation: 'En primer lugar' formal for introducing first point. Academic/professional discourse structure.

items.question: _____, debemos considerar las implicaciones financieras de esta decisión empresarial.
items.translation: (On the other hand, we must consider the financial implications of this business decision)
items.explanation: 'Por otro lado' introduces contrasting perspective. Presenting alternative viewpoint.

items.question: _____, quiero agradecer a todos los que contribuyeron al éxito del proyecto.
items.translation: (Finally, I want to thank everyone who contributed to the project's success)
items.explanation: 'Por último' formal conclusion marker. Professional closing of discourse.

items.question: Los resultados fueron positivos; _____, debemos mantener la cautela en el análisis.
items.translation: (The results were positive; however, we must maintain caution in the analysis)
items.explanation: 'Sin embargo' formal adversative connector. Academic caution despite positive results.

items.question: _____, me parece importante resaltar que este método tiene limitaciones claras.
items.translation: (In addition, it seems important to me to highlight this method has clear limitations)
items.explanation: 'Asimismo' very formal addition marker. Academic discourse adding related point.

items.question: El proyecto es viable; _____, requiere una inversión inicial significativa y sostenida.
items.translation: (The project is viable; nevertheless, it requires a significant and sustained initial investment)
items.explanation: 'No obstante' formal concession marker. Acknowledging drawback despite viability.

items.question: _____, podemos concluir que la hipótesis planteada era correcta desde el principio.
items.translation: (In conclusion, we can conclude the stated hypothesis was correct from the beginning)
items.explanation: 'En conclusión' formal ending marker. Academic final statement introduction.

items.question: _____ que hemos analizado, los datos sugieren una tendencia clara y consistente.
items.translation: (According to what we've analyzed, the data suggest a clear and consistent trend)
items.explanation: 'Según lo que' references prior analysis. Basing conclusion on evidence discussed.

items.question: _____ una cosa, _____ otra, el resultado final fue positivo para todos.
items.translation: (Between one thing and another, the final result was positive for everyone)
items.explanation: 'Entre una cosa y otra' means between various factors. Acknowledging multiple influences.

items.question: _____ de vista personal, considero que esta es la mejor opción disponible.
items.translation: (From a personal point of view, I consider this is the best available option)
items.explanation: 'Desde el punto de vista' introduces perspective. Framing opinion source.

items.question: _____ palabras, necesitamos más tiempo para completar adecuadamente el análisis requerido.
items.translation: (In other words, we need more time to adequately complete the required analysis)
items.explanation: 'En otras palabras' rephrases for clarity. Reformulating for better understanding.

items.question: _____ todo lo expuesto anteriormente, propongo que aceptemos la oferta recibida.
items.translation: (Given everything stated previously, I propose we accept the received offer)
items.explanation: 'Dado' introduces basis for conclusion. Formal reasoning connector.

items.question: _____ de lo contrario, procederemos según lo planeado originalmente en la reunión.
items.translation: (Unless otherwise indicated, we'll proceed according to what was originally planned in the meeting)
items.explanation: 'A menos que' in formal 'salvo indicación contraria'. Business contingency language.

items.question: _____ lugar a dudas, este es el mejor curso de acción dadas las circunstancias.
items.translation: (Without a doubt, this is the best course of action given the circumstances)
items.explanation: 'Sin lugar a dudas' formal certainty expression. Emphatic confidence marker.

items.question: _____ resumidas cuentas, el balance general del año fiscal fue muy positivo.
items.translation: (In short, the overall balance of the fiscal year was very positive)
items.explanation: 'En resumidas cuentas' formal summary phrase. Concluding financial discussion.

name: Subjunctive - Opinion and Doubt
description: Certainty vs uncertainty in complex contexts

items.question: Estoy convencido de que ella _____ la mejor candidata para dirigir el departamento.
items.translation: (I'm convinced she is the best candidate to lead the department)
items.explanation: Strong conviction uses indicative. 'Estar convencido que' expresses certainty requiring indicative.

items.question: No estoy convencido de que esta _____ la solución más adecuada al problema.
items.translation: (I'm not convinced this is the most adequate solution to the problem)
items.explanation: Negative conviction triggers subjunctive. Lack of certainty requires subjunctive mood.

items.question: Me parece evidente que ellos no _____ suficiente experiencia en este campo específico.
items.translation: (It seems evident to me they don't have enough experience in this specific field)
items.explanation: 'Me parece evidente que' expresses certainty with indicative. Clear observation uses indicative.

items.question: No me parece que _____ justo castigar a todos por el error de uno solo.
items.translation: (It doesn't seem fair to me to punish everyone for one person's mistake)
items.explanation: Negative 'no parece que' triggers subjunctive. Questioning fairness requires subjunctive.

items.question: Es obvio que tú _____ mucho esfuerzo en preparar esta presentación profesional.
items.translation: (It's obvious you put a lot of effort into preparing this professional presentation)
items.explanation: 'Es obvio que' expresses certainty with indicative. Evident fact uses indicative.

items.question: No es obvio para mí que esta _____ la única alternativa viable que tenemos.
items.translation: (It's not obvious to me this is the only viable alternative we have)
items.explanation: Negative certainty expression triggers subjunctive. Questioning obviousness requires subjunctive.

items.question: Supongo que ya _____ las instrucciones detalladas que te envié por correo.
items.translation: (I suppose you already received the detailed instructions I sent you by email)
items.explanation: 'Suponer que' mild assumption uses indicative. Supposing probable fact uses indicative.

items.question: No es que no me _____ tu propuesta, sino que necesito más tiempo para considerarla.
items.translation: (It's not that I don't like your proposal, but rather I need more time to consider it)
items.explanation: 'No es que' always requires subjunctive. Clarifying misunderstanding with subjunctive.

items.question: Es indudable que el cambio climático _____ un problema global muy serio.
items.translation: (It's undeniable climate change is a very serious global problem)
items.explanation: 'Es indudable que' expresses absolute certainty. Indisputable facts use indicative.

items.question: Puede ser que _____ razón en tu análisis detallado de la situación actual.
items.translation: (It may be you're right in your detailed analysis of the current situation)
items.explanation: 'Puede ser que' expresses possibility with subjunctive. Acknowledging potential correctness.

items.question: Está claro que necesitamos _____ más recursos humanos para este proyecto ambicioso.
items.translation: (It's clear we need to hire more human resources for this ambitious project)
items.explanation: 'Está claro que necesitamos' + infinitive. After expression of need, infinitive is natural.

items.question: No está nada claro que ellos _____ cumplir con los plazos establecidos firmemente.
items.translation: (It's not at all clear they can meet the firmly established deadlines)
items.explanation: Negative clarity triggers subjunctive. Uncertainty about capability requires subjunctive.

items.question: Doy por sentado que todos _____ leído el informe antes de esta reunión importante.
items.translation: (I take for granted everyone has read the report before this important meeting)
items.explanation: 'Dar por sentado que' assumes fact with indicative. Taking something as given uses indicative.

items.question: No doy por sentado que _____ de acuerdo con mi punto de vista personal.
items.translation: (I don't take for granted you agree with my personal point of view)
items.explanation: Negative assumption triggers subjunctive. Not presuming agreement requires subjunctive.

items.question: Tengo la certeza absoluta de que este proyecto _____ un éxito rotundo en el mercado.
items.translation: (I have absolute certainty this project will be a resounding success in the market)
items.explanation: 'Tener certeza de que' expresses conviction with indicative. Certainty about future uses future indicative.

name: Pronominal Verbs
description: Verbs that change meaning with reflexive pronouns

items.question: Me _____ de todos mis viejos amigos del colegio con mucho cariño y nostalgia.
items.translation: (I remember all my old school friends with great affection and nostalgia)
items.explanation: 'Acordarse de' means remember (requires 'de'). Different from 'recordar' which doesn't need 'de'.

items.question: Ella se _____ en su nuevo trabajo después de solo tres meses de esfuerzo constante.
items.translation: (She became established in her new job after only three months of constant effort)
items.explanation: 'Establecerse' means become established/settle. Reflexive shows self-positioning in role.

items.question: Me _____ cuenta inmediatamente de que algo extraño estaba ocurriendo en la oficina.
items.translation: (I realized immediately something strange was happening in the office)
items.explanation: 'Darse cuenta de' means realize. Fixed reflexive expression for awareness.

items.question: Se _____ a llorar desconsoladamente cuando escuchó las terribles noticias familiares.
items.translation: (She burst out crying inconsolably when she heard the terrible family news)
items.explanation: 'Ponerse a' + infinitive means start suddenly. Abrupt beginning of emotional reaction.

items.question: Nos _____ en el café de siempre a las cinco de la tarde puntualmente.
items.translation: (We met at the usual café at five in the afternoon punctually)
items.explanation: 'Encontrarse' means meet by arrangement. Reciprocal meeting at agreed place.

items.question: Se _____ con todas sus fuerzas para lograr su sueño de ser atleta olímpico.
items.translation: (He strove with all his strength to achieve his dream of being an Olympic athlete)
items.explanation: 'Esforzarse' means strive/make effort. Reflexive intensifies personal exertion.

items.question: Me _____ de menos mi ciudad natal cada vez que estoy lejos por trabajo.
items.translation: (I miss my hometown every time I'm away for work)
items.explanation: 'Echar de menos' means miss someone/something. Standard expression for longing.

items.question: Se _____ muy triste cuando sus mejores amigos se mudaron al extranjero.
items.translation: (He became very sad when his best friends moved abroad)
items.explanation: 'Ponerse' for sudden emotional change. Quick shift to sadness.

items.question: Nos _____ profundamente del comportamiento grosero que tuvimos con ustedes.
items.translation: (We deeply regret the rude behavior we had with you)
items.explanation: 'Arrepentirse de' means regret/repent. Deep remorse for past actions.

items.question: Me _____ completamente en blanco durante el examen oral por los nervios.
items.translation: (I went completely blank during the oral exam because of nerves)
items.explanation: 'Quedarse en blanco' means go blank/forget. Mental block under pressure.

items.question: Se _____ a estudiar seriamente después de reprobar el primer examen importante.
items.translation: (He got down to studying seriously after failing the first important exam)
items.explanation: 'Ponerse a' + infinitive means begin seriously. Starting focused activity.

items.question: Me _____ muy orgulloso cuando mi hija se graduó con honores de la universidad.
items.translation: (I felt very proud when my daughter graduated with honors from university)
items.explanation: 'Sentirse' expresses emotional state felt. Internal emotional experience.

items.question: Se _____ loco de contento al enterarse de que había ganado la beca completa.
items.translation: (He went crazy with joy upon finding out he had won the full scholarship)
items.explanation: 'Volverse loco' means go crazy. Extreme emotional reaction to news.

items.question: Nos _____ en contacto regular después de terminar nuestros estudios universitarios juntos.
items.translation: (We stayed in regular contact after finishing our university studies together)
items.explanation: 'Mantenerse en contacto' means stay in touch. Continuing communication relationship.

items.question: Se _____ cuenta tarde de que había cometido un error grave e irreversible.
items.translation: (He realized late he had made a serious and irreversible mistake)
items.explanation: 'Darse cuenta' for realization. Becoming aware of situation or truth.

name: Advanced Negative Expressions
description: Complex negation patterns and double negatives

items.question: No he visto a _____ en toda la tarde aquí en la oficina central.
items.translation: (I haven't seen anyone all afternoon here in the central office)
items.explanation: After negative verb, use negative indefinite. Double negative is required in Spanish.

items.question: _____ ha llamado por teléfono en todo el día desde temprano esta mañana.
items.translation: (Nobody has called on the phone all day since early this morning)
items.explanation: 'Nadie' as subject doesn't need 'no'. Negative word before verb makes verb affirmative.

items.question: No tengo _____ interés en participar en ese proyecto tan poco claro.
items.translation: (I have no interest whatsoever in participating in that very unclear project)
items.explanation: After negative, 'ningún' before masculine noun. Absolute lack of interest.

items.question: _____ me dijo _____ sobre los cambios importantes en la empresa ayer.
items.translation: (Nobody told me anything about the important changes in the company yesterday)
items.explanation: Double negative required. 'Nadie' subject with 'nada' object, no 'no' needed.

items.question: No quiero _____ trato especial, trátame como a cualquier otro empleado normal.
items.translation: (I don't want any special treatment, treat me like any other normal employee)
items.explanation: After 'no quiero', use 'ningún'. Rejecting special consideration entirely.

items.question: _____ iré _____ a ese lugar horrible después de lo que me pasó.
items.translation: (I'll never go back to that horrible place after what happened to me)
items.explanation: 'Nunca más' means never again. Emphatic refusal to return.

items.question: No he estado _____ tan nervioso en toda mi vida profesional hasta ahora.
items.translation: (I've never been so nervous in my entire professional life until now)
items.explanation: 'No...nunca' emphasizes negative. Redundant negation for emphasis in Spanish.

items.question: _____ le he contado a _____ sobre mis planes secretos de mudanza.
items.translation: (I haven't told anyone about my secret moving plans)
items.explanation: 'No' + verb + 'nadie' standard negative pattern. Double negative for complete denial.

items.question: En mi vida he visto _____ tan hermoso como este paisaje natural impresionante.
items.translation: (Never in my life have I seen anything as beautiful as this impressive natural landscape)
items.explanation: 'En mi vida' implies negation, use 'nada'. Lifetime superlative with negative.

items.question: No voy a _____ a ese restaurante caro después de la mala experiencia pasada.
items.translation: (I'm not going to that expensive restaurant ever again after the bad past experience)
items.explanation: 'No volver nunca más' emphatic refusal. Multiple negatives emphasize determination.

items.question: _____ tengo _____ ganas de discutir contigo sobre este tema tan delicado.
items.translation: (I have absolutely no desire to argue with you about this very delicate topic)
items.explanation: 'No tener ningunas ganas' emphatic unwillingness. Feminine plural intensifies rejection.

items.question: _____ dijo _____ palabra durante toda la reunión tensa que duró horas.
items.translation: (He didn't say a single word during the entire tense meeting that lasted hours)
items.explanation: 'No...ni una palabra' emphasizes complete silence. Total absence of speech.

items.question: No me gusta _____ de lo que propusiste en tu presentación de ayer.
items.translation: (I don't like anything about what you proposed in your presentation yesterday)
items.explanation: 'No me gusta nada de' complete rejection. Nothing appealing in proposal.

items.question: _____ vez en mi carrera profesional había enfrentado un desafío tan grande.
items.translation: (Never in my professional career had I faced such a great challenge)
items.explanation: 'Nunca' before verb, no 'no' needed. Negative word as subject makes verb affirmative.

items.question: No conozco a _____ de las personas que asistieron a esa conferencia internacional.
items.translation: (I don't know any of the people who attended that international conference)
items.explanation: 'No...ninguna de' for total absence. Not knowing any single person.

name: Cuando - Indicative vs Subjunctive
description: Master when to use each mood with cuando

items.question: Cuando _____ pequeño en mi infancia, jugaba todo el día en la calle con amigos.
items.translation: (When I was little in my childhood, I played all day in the street with friends)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with factual past uses imperfect indicative. Known past state uses indicative.

items.question: Cuando _____ mayor y tenga mi propio dinero, viajaré por todo el mundo.
items.translation: (When I'm older and have my own money, I'll travel all over the world)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with future reference requires subjunctive. Uncertain future time uses subjunctive.

items.question: Cuando _____ ayer a casa del trabajo, me encontré con mi antiguo profesor.
items.translation: (When I returned home from work yesterday, I ran into my old teacher)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with completed specific past uses preterite. Definite past moment uses indicative.

items.question: Te llamaré cuando _____ del médico esta tarde con los resultados de análisis.
items.translation: (I'll call you when I get back from the doctor this afternoon with the test results)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with pending future uses subjunctive. Planned future call after uncertain return.

items.question: Cuando _____ en el extranjero hace años, aprendí mucho sobre otras culturas.
items.translation: (When I lived abroad years ago, I learned a lot about other cultures)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with habitual past uses imperfect indicative. Repeated past state uses indicative.

items.question: Cuando finalmente _____ el proyecto completo, celebraremos con una gran fiesta.
items.translation: (When we finally finish the complete project, we'll celebrate with a big party)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with future completion uses subjunctive. Anticipated finish requires subjunctive.

items.question: Cuando _____ la noticia impactante ayer, no supe qué decir ni hacer.
items.translation: (When I heard the shocking news yesterday, I didn't know what to say or do)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with specific past moment uses preterite. Definite past event uses indicative.

items.question: Cuando _____ en el mismo edificio que mi oficina, caminaba al trabajo cada día.
items.translation: (When I lived in the same building as my office, I walked to work every day)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' describing habitual past uses imperfect. Routine past situation uses indicative.

items.question: Avísame cuando _____ listo para salir de casa hacia el aeropuerto internacional.
items.translation: (Let me know when you're ready to leave home for the international airport)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with future readiness uses subjunctive. Unknown timing of readiness.

items.question: Cuando _____ ese programa en la televisión, siempre me hace reír muchísimo.
items.translation: (When I watch that program on television, it always makes me laugh a lot)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with habitual present uses indicative. Regular occurrence uses indicative.

items.question: Cuando tú _____ mañana, yo ya habré salido para el trabajo temprano.
items.translation: (When you wake up tomorrow, I will have already left for work early)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with tomorrow's action uses subjunctive. Future morning event requires subjunctive.

items.question: Cuando él _____ la verdad completa hace años, todo cambió en su vida.
items.translation: (When he discovered the complete truth years ago, everything changed in his life)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with definite past discovery uses preterite. Life-changing past moment uses indicative.

items.question: Cuando _____ suficiente experiencia acumulada, podrás solicitar ese ascenso laboral.
items.translation: (When you have enough accumulated experience, you'll be able to apply for that job promotion)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with future acquisition uses subjunctive. Uncertain timing of readiness.

items.question: Cuando _____ joven y sin responsabilidades, viajé por toda Europa sin planificar.
items.translation: (When I was young and without responsibilities, I traveled all over Europe without planning)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' describing past youth uses imperfect. Known past state of being.

items.question: Cuando _____ todos los requisitos necesarios, podrás graduarte oficialmente de la universidad.
items.translation: (When you complete all the necessary requirements, you'll be able to officially graduate from university)
items.explanation: 'Cuando' with future fulfillment uses subjunctive. Uncertain timing of completion.

items.question: _____ a los datos estadísticos presentados, se observa una tendencia clara.
items.translation: (With respect to the statistical data presented, a clear trend is observed)
items.explanation: 'Con respecto a' formal regarding phrase. Academic reference to data.

items.question: _____ se puede apreciar en el gráfico adjunto, los resultados son concluyentes.
items.translation: (As can be appreciated in the attached graph, the results are conclusive)
items.explanation: 'Como se puede apreciar' formal observation phrase. Directing attention to evidence.

items.question: _____ efectos prácticos, consideraremos ambas opciones como igualmente válidas.
items.translation: (For practical purposes, we'll consider both options as equally valid)
items.explanation: 'A efectos de' formal purpose phrase. Legal/academic specification of intent.

items.question: _____ lo anterior, resulta evidente que se requieren más investigaciones profundas.
items.translation: (In view of the above, it's evident more in-depth research is required)
items.explanation: 'En vista de' formal considering phrase. Drawing conclusions from prior evidence.

items.question: Los resultados obtenidos se encuentran _____ concordancia con estudios previos realizados.
items.translation: (The obtained results are in agreement with previous studies conducted)
items.explanation: 'En concordancia con' formal agreement phrase. Academic alignment with literature.

items.question: _____ subrayar la importancia fundamental de estos hallazgos para la ciencia.
items.translation: (It's worth emphasizing the fundamental importance of these findings for science)
items.explanation: 'Cabe subrayar' academic emphasis phrase. Scholarly highlighting of significance.

items.question: _____ al tema principal de nuestra discusión, debemos tomar una decisión.
items.translation: (Returning to the main topic of our discussion, we must make a decision)
items.explanation: 'Volviendo a' refocuses discussion. Professional meeting redirection.

items.question: _____ marco de este estudio científico, analizamos cincuenta casos detalladamente.
items.translation: (Within the framework of this scientific study, we analyzed fifty cases in detail)
items.explanation: 'En el marco de' formal scope phrase. Academic context specification.

items.question: _____ que antecede, se propone implementar las siguientes medidas correctivas.
items.translation: (Based on what precedes, it's proposed to implement the following corrective measures)
items.explanation: 'Dado lo que antecede' formal reasoning phrase. Legal/academic argumentation.

items.question: _____ lugar a dudas razonables, estos resultados demuestran la validez de la teoría.
items.translation: (Without room for reasonable doubts, these results demonstrate the theory's validity)
items.explanation: 'Sin lugar a dudas' formal certainty phrase. Academic confidence expression.

items.question: Los datos _____ la necesidad imperativa de modificar nuestra estrategia actual.
items.translation: (The data point to the imperative need to modify our current strategy)
items.explanation: 'Apuntar a' formal indication phrase. Data suggesting direction.

items.question: _____ de la investigación exhaustiva, podemos afirmar con certeza razonable que...
items.translation: (Based on the exhaustive investigation, we can state with reasonable certainty that...)
items.explanation: 'A partir de' formal basis phrase. Foundation for conclusions.

items.question: _____ destacar que estos resultados deben interpretarse con prudencia metodológica.
items.translation: (It's worth highlighting these results must be interpreted with methodological prudence)
items.explanation: 'Conviene destacar' academic advisement. Scholarly caution recommendation.

items.question: _____ tenor de los hallazgos, se sugiere continuar con investigaciones adicionales.
items.translation: (In light of the findings, it's suggested to continue with additional investigations)
items.explanation: 'A tenor de' very formal consideration phrase. High-level academic reasoning.

items.question: _____ modo de conclusión final, cabe afirmar que la hipótesis fue validada.
items.translation: (By way of final conclusion, it can be stated the hypothesis was validated)
items.explanation: 'A modo de' formal framing phrase. Introducing concluding statement type.

name: Ser vs Estar - Contextual Nuances
description: Subtle meaning changes with different verbs

items.question: Mi abuela _____ muy joven cuando se casó con mi abuelo en la posguerra.
items.translation: (My grandmother was very young when she married my grandfather in the postwar period)
items.explanation: 'Ser joven' describes inherent age characteristic. Permanent state at that life stage.

items.question: Mi abuela _____ muy joven y radiante en las fotos de su boda antigua.
items.translation: (My grandmother looked very young and radiant in her old wedding photos)
items.explanation: 'Estar joven' describes appearance in specific moment. How she looked in photo.

items.question: Estas manzanas _____ deliciosas, tienen un sabor dulce natural incomparable.
items.translation: (These apples are delicious, they have an incomparable natural sweet flavor)
items.explanation: 'Ser delicioso' describes inherent quality. Natural characteristic of apple type.

items.question: Estas manzanas _____ deliciosas hoy porque las acaban de cosechar frescas.
items.translation: (These apples are delicious today because they just harvested them fresh)
items.explanation: 'Estar delicioso' describes current state. Temporary peak freshness condition.

items.question: El director general _____ muy ocupado siempre con sus múltiples responsabilidades importantes.
items.translation: (The general manager is always very busy with his multiple important responsibilities)
items.explanation: 'Ser ocupado' describes characteristic tendency. Habitually busy as personality trait.

items.question: El director _____ muy ocupado ahora mismo, ¿puede volver en una hora?
items.translation: (The director is very busy right now, can you come back in an hour?)
items.explanation: 'Estar ocupado' describes current temporary state. Busy at this specific moment.

items.question: Mi hermano _____ muy listo y siempre saca las mejores notas sin estudiar mucho.
items.translation: (My brother is very smart and always gets the best grades without studying much)
items.explanation: 'Ser listo' means be smart/clever. Inherent intelligence as characteristic.

items.question: Ya _____ listo para salir, solo necesito ponerme los zapatos y la chaqueta.
items.translation: (I'm already ready to leave, I just need to put on my shoes and jacket)
items.explanation: 'Estar listo' means be ready/prepared. Temporary state of readiness.

items.question: Ese actor famoso _____ muy aburrido como persona en la vida real privada.
items.translation: (That famous actor is very boring as a person in real private life)
items.explanation: 'Ser aburrido' means be boring (inherent trait). Personality characteristic.

items.question: _____ muy aburrido en esta fiesta tan monótona, ¿podemos irnos ya?
items.translation: (I'm very bored at this very monotonous party, can we leave now?)
items.explanation: 'Estar aburrido' means feel bored. Temporary emotional state of boredom.

items.question: La situación _____ grave y requiere nuestra atención inmediata y decisiva.
items.translation: (The situation is serious and requires our immediate and decisive attention)
items.explanation: 'Ser grave' for inherent seriousness. Situation's nature is serious.

items.question: Mi padre _____ grave en el hospital después del accidente automovilístico severo.
items.translation: (My father is in serious condition in the hospital after the severe car accident)
items.explanation: 'Estar grave' for health condition. Temporary critical medical state.

items.question: Esta sopa _____ muy rica, tiene un sabor casero tradicional auténtico.
items.translation: (This soup is very tasty, it has an authentic traditional homemade flavor)
items.explanation: 'Estar rico' for food taste. Current delicious state of prepared food.

items.question: Él _____ un hombre muy rico que posee varias propiedades inmobiliarias importantes.
items.translation: (He is a very rich man who owns several important real estate properties)
items.explanation: 'Ser rico' for wealth status. Inherent financial characteristic.

items.question: Esa profesora _____ muy buena explicando conceptos matemáticos complejos a estudiantes.
items.translation: (That teacher is very good at explaining complex mathematical concepts to students)
items.explanation: 'Ser bueno' for inherent skill. Characteristic teaching ability.

items.question: Dejé _____ fumar hace exactamente tres años por razones de salud importantes.
items.translation: (I stopped smoking exactly three years ago for important health reasons)
items.explanation: 'Dejar de' + infinitive means stop doing. Required preposition for cessation.

items.question: Empecé _____ trabajar en esta empresa cuando tenía solo veintidós años.
items.translation: (I started working at this company when I was only twenty-two years old)
items.explanation: 'Empezar a' + infinitive means begin to. Required 'a' for starting action.

items.question: Me olvidé completamente _____ comprar leche en el supermercado esta mañana.
items.translation: (I completely forgot to buy milk at the supermarket this morning)
items.explanation: 'Olvidarse de' requires 'de'. Forgetting to do something needs preposition.

items.question: Insistió _____ pagar toda la cuenta completa del restaurante caro ayer.
items.translation: (He insisted on paying the entire bill at the expensive restaurant yesterday)
items.explanation: 'Insistir en' requires 'en'. Persistence about action needs this preposition.

items.question: Sueño _____ viajar algún día a Japón y conocer su cultura fascinante.
items.translation: (I dream of traveling someday to Japan and getting to know its fascinating culture)
items.explanation: 'Soñar con' means dream of/about. Dreams and aspirations use 'con'.

items.question: Acabamos _____ llegar del aeropuerto después de un vuelo internacional agotador.
items.translation: (We just arrived from the airport after an exhausting international flight)
items.explanation: 'Acabar de' + infinitive means just finished. Recent completion requires 'de'.

items.question: Se negó rotundamente _____ aceptar las nuevas condiciones laborales propuestas.
items.translation: (He categorically refused to accept the proposed new labor conditions)
items.explanation: 'Negarse a' means refuse to. Refusal to act requires 'a'.

items.question: Aprendí _____ tocar la guitarra clásica cuando era adolescente en el instituto.
items.translation: (I learned to play classical guitar when I was a teenager in high school)
items.explanation: 'Aprender a' + infinitive means learn to. Acquiring skill requires 'a'.

items.question: Me atreví finalmente _____ decirle la verdad completa sobre mis sentimientos.
items.translation: (I finally dared to tell her the complete truth about my feelings)
items.explanation: 'Atreverse a' means dare to. Courage to act requires 'a'.

items.question: Tardé mucho tiempo _____ entender completamente las reglas complicadas del juego.
items.translation: (It took me a long time to completely understand the complicated rules of the game)
items.explanation: 'Tardar en' means take time to. Duration before accomplishment uses 'en'.

items.question: Quedamos _____ vernos mañana a las tres en punto en el café del centro.
items.translation: (We agreed to see each other tomorrow at three o'clock sharp at the downtown café)
items.explanation: 'Quedar en' means agree to/arrange. Making plans requires 'en'.

items.question: Ayúdame _____ cargar todas estas cajas pesadas hasta el coche estacionado afuera.
items.translation: (Help me carry all these heavy boxes to the car parked outside)
items.explanation: 'Ayudar a' someone to do something. Assistance with action requires 'a'.

items.question: Renunció definitivamente _____ su puesto directivo después de veinte años de servicio.
items.translation: (He definitively resigned from his managerial position after twenty years of service)
items.explanation: 'Renunciar a' means resign from. Giving up position requires 'a'.

items.question: Pensamos seriamente _____ mudarnos a otra ciudad más tranquila el próximo año.
items.translation: (We're seriously thinking about moving to another calmer city next year)
items.explanation: 'Pensar en' means think about. Considering action requires 'en'.

items.question: Confío plenamente _____ que tú harás lo correcto en esta situación tan delicada.
items.translation: (I fully trust you'll do the right thing in this very delicate situation)
items.explanation: 'Confiar en' means trust in. Faith in person or outcome uses 'en'.

items.question: Es fundamental que todos _____ puntualmente a la reunión importante de mañana.
items.translation: (It's fundamental everyone arrives punctually to tomorrow's important meeting)
items.explanation: 'Es fundamental que' expresses necessity with subjunctive. Important requirement needs subjunctive.

items.question: Es probable que _____ mañana según el pronóstico meteorológico actualizado.
items.translation: (It's probable it will rain tomorrow according to the updated weather forecast)
items.explanation: 'Es probable que' expresses likelihood with subjunctive. Probability requires subjunctive mood.

items.question: Es cierto que los precios _____ subido considerablemente en los últimos meses.
items.translation: (It's certain prices have risen considerably in the last months)
items.explanation: 'Es cierto que' states fact with indicative. Certainty about reality uses indicative.

items.question: Es importante que _____ esta información confidencial en secreto absoluto.
items.translation: (It's important you keep this confidential information in absolute secrecy)
items.explanation: 'Es importante que' triggers subjunctive for importance. Crucial action requires subjunctive.

items.question: Es verdad que el proyecto _____ más tiempo del originalmente estimado.
items.translation: (It's true the project took more time than originally estimated)
items.explanation: 'Es verdad que' affirms fact with indicative. Truth statement uses indicative.

items.question: Es necesario que _____ más atención a los detalles pequeños pero importantes.
items.translation: (It's necessary we pay more attention to small but important details)
items.explanation: 'Es necesario que' expresses need with subjunctive. Necessity requires subjunctive mood.

items.question: Es evidente que ella _____ mucho talento natural para la música clásica.
items.translation: (It's evident she has a lot of natural talent for classical music)
items.explanation: 'Es evidente que' states obvious fact. Evident truth uses indicative.

items.question: Es posible que ellos _____ cambiar de opinión después de escuchar los argumentos.
items.translation: (It's possible they'll change their opinion after hearing the arguments)
items.explanation: 'Es posible que' expresses possibility with subjunctive. Maybe-situation requires subjunctive.

items.question: Es una lástima que no _____ venir con nosotros al viaje maravilloso.
items.translation: (It's a shame you can't come with us on the wonderful trip)
items.explanation: 'Es una lástima que' expresses regret with subjunctive. Pity about situation requires subjunctive.

items.question: Es increíble que ya _____ pasado diez años desde que nos graduamos juntos.
items.translation: (It's incredible ten years have already passed since we graduated together)
items.explanation: 'Es increíble que' can use subjunctive for amazement. Though fact, emotion triggers subjunctive.

items.question: Es mejor que _____ inmediatamente antes de que sea demasiado tarde para actuar.
items.translation: (It's better we leave immediately before it's too late to act)
items.explanation: 'Es mejor que' gives advice with subjunctive. Recommendation requires subjunctive mood.

items.question: Es seguro que _____ muchos desafíos difíciles en el camino hacia el éxito.
items.translation: (It's certain there will be many difficult challenges on the path to success)
items.explanation: 'Es seguro que' expresses certainty with indicative. Sure future uses future indicative.

items.question: Es imprescindible que _____ todos los documentos requeridos antes del viernes próximo.
items.translation: (It's essential you bring all required documents before next Friday)
items.explanation: 'Es imprescindible que' expresses absolute necessity. Essential requirement needs subjunctive.

items.question: Es lógico que _____ cansado después de trabajar doce horas seguidas sin parar.
items.translation: (It's logical you're tired after working twelve hours straight without stopping)
items.explanation: 'Es lógico que' can use subjunctive. Though logical, expressing understanding uses subjunctive.

items.question: Es un hecho comprobado que la Tierra _____ alrededor del Sol constantemente.
items.translation: (It's a proven fact the Earth revolves around the Sun constantly)
items.explanation: 'Es un hecho que' states scientific truth. Indisputable facts use indicative.

name: Purpose Clauses - Para que, A fin de que
description: Express intentions and goals with subjunctive

items.question: Te explico todo esto detalladamente para que _____ bien la situación compleja.
items.translation: (I'm explaining all this to you in detail so you understand the complex situation well)
items.explanation: 'Para que' always triggers subjunctive. Purpose of explaining requires subjunctive.

items.question: Trabajaré horas extras este mes a fin de que mi familia _____ vivir más cómodamente.
items.translation: (I'll work overtime this month so that my family can live more comfortably)
items.explanation: 'A fin de que' formal purpose clause with subjunctive. Goal for family requires subjunctive.

items.question: Le presté dinero para que _____ pagar sus deudas urgentes del banco.
items.translation: (I lent him money so he could pay his urgent bank debts)
items.explanation: 'Para que' in past context uses imperfect subjunctive. Past purpose requires past subjunctive.

items.question: Hablo despacio y claro para que todos me _____ entender perfectamente bien.
items.translation: (I speak slowly and clearly so everyone can understand me perfectly well)
items.explanation: 'Para que' with plural subject. Purpose for group understanding uses subjunctive.

items.question: Te doy mi número personal para que me _____ contactar cuando lo necesites urgentemente.
items.translation: (I'm giving you my personal number so you can contact me when you urgently need to)
items.explanation: 'Para que' enabling future contact. Purpose of giving information requires subjunctive.

items.question: Guardé todos los documentos importantes a fin de que no se _____ perder nunca.
items.translation: (I kept all important documents so they wouldn't get lost ever)
items.explanation: 'A fin de que' in past with negative purpose. Preventing loss requires past subjunctive.

items.question: Voy a repetir las instrucciones para que no _____ ninguna confusión posible.
items.translation: (I'm going to repeat the instructions so there's no possible confusion)
items.explanation: 'Para que' with negative purpose. Preventing confusion requires subjunctive.

items.question: Le mandé el correo electrónico para que _____ la información antes de la junta.
items.translation: (I sent him the email so he would have the information before the meeting)
items.explanation: 'Para que' in past context requires imperfect subjunctive. Past purpose for past possession.

items.question: Encendí todas las luces del jardín para que los invitados _____ ver bien el camino.
items.translation: (I turned on all the garden lights so guests could see the path well)
items.explanation: Past 'para que' uses imperfect subjunctive. Past action for past purpose.

items.question: Estudio mucho cada noche para que mis padres _____ orgullosos de mí.
items.translation: (I study a lot every night so my parents are proud of me)
items.explanation: 'Para que' with emotional goal. Purpose of causing pride requires subjunctive.

items.question: Bajé el volumen de la música para que los vecinos no se _____ molestar.
items.translation: (I lowered the music volume so the neighbors wouldn't get bothered)
items.explanation: Past 'para que' with negative purpose uses past subjunctive. Preventing annoyance.

items.question: Reservé con anticipación para que _____ sitio disponible en el restaurante concurrido.
items.translation: (I reserved in advance so there would be space available in the crowded restaurant)
items.explanation: Past reservation with past purpose uses imperfect subjunctive. Ensuring past availability.

items.question: Les estoy enviando instrucciones detalladas para que _____ exactamente qué hacer.
items.translation: (I'm sending you detailed instructions so you know exactly what to do)
items.explanation: 'Para que' with knowledge goal. Purpose of providing clarity requires subjunctive.

items.question: Compré un coche grande a fin de que toda mi familia _____ viajar cómodamente junta.
items.translation: (I bought a big car so my entire family could travel comfortably together)
items.explanation: Past purchase for past purpose uses imperfect subjunctive. Enabling family comfort.

items.question: Voy a grabar la conferencia entera para que tú la _____ escuchar después.
items.translation: (I'm going to record the entire conference so you can listen to it later)
items.explanation: 'Para que' enabling future listening. Purpose of recording requires subjunctive.

name: Probability and Possibility Expressions
description: Tal vez, quizás, probablemente with mood changes

items.question: Tal vez _____ a visitarte este fin de semana si tengo tiempo libre disponible.
items.translation: (Perhaps I'll come visit you this weekend if I have free time available)
items.explanation: 'Tal vez' with subjunctive emphasizes uncertainty. Maybe-visit uses subjunctive for doubt.

items.question: Quizás ellos no _____ enterado todavía de las noticias importantes de hoy.
items.translation: (Perhaps they haven't found out yet about today's important news)
items.explanation: 'Quizás' with present perfect subjunctive for uncertain past. Maybe they know, maybe not.

items.question: Probablemente _____ mañana si el pronóstico del tiempo resulta correcto finalmente.
items.translation: (It will probably rain tomorrow if the weather forecast turns out correct finally)
items.explanation: 'Probablemente' can use subjunctive for emphasis. Though likely, subjunctive shows remaining doubt.

items.question: Posiblemente _____ que cambiar nuestros planes de vacaciones por el mal tiempo.
items.translation: (We'll possibly have to change our vacation plans because of the bad weather)
items.explanation: 'Posiblemente' with subjunctive emphasizes uncertainty. Possible need for change.

items.question: Acaso _____ la verdad escondida si investigamos más profundamente el caso.
items.translation: (Perhaps we'll discover the hidden truth if we investigate the case more deeply)
items.explanation: 'Acaso' literary probability uses subjunctive. Uncertain discovery requires subjunctive.

items.question: A lo mejor _____ que posponer la boda por la situación familiar complicada.
items.translation: (Maybe we'll have to postpone the wedding because of the complicated family situation)
items.explanation: 'A lo mejor' always uses indicative. Colloquial maybe uses indicative, unlike 'tal vez'.

items.question: Puede que _____ razón en tu análisis crítico de la situación económica.
items.translation: (It may be you're right in your critical analysis of the economic situation)
items.explanation: 'Puede que' always requires subjunctive. Acknowledging possible correctness.

items.question: Seguramente ya _____ de comer cuando lleguemos al restaurante a las nueve.
items.translation: (They've surely already eaten when we arrive at the restaurant at nine)
items.explanation: 'Seguramente' with high certainty uses future perfect indicative. Confident prediction uses indicative.

items.question: Es probable que para esa fecha ya _____ mudado a nuestra casa nueva.
items.translation: (It's probable that by that date we'll have already moved to our new house)
items.explanation: 'Es probable que' with future completion uses present perfect subjunctive. Likely future state.

items.question: Quizás no _____ sido buena idea invertir todo nuestro dinero en ese negocio.
items.translation: (Perhaps it wasn't a good idea to invest all our money in that business)
items.explanation: 'Quizás' with past uses present perfect subjunctive. Doubting past decision quality.

items.question: Tal vez _____ demasiado optimistas en nuestras proyecciones financieras iniciales.
items.translation: (Perhaps we were too optimistic in our initial financial projections)
items.explanation: 'Tal vez' with known past can use indicative. Reflecting on definite past attitude.

items.question: Puede ser que mañana _____ mejor día para tener esa conversación difícil.
items.translation: (It may be tomorrow is a better day to have that difficult conversation)
items.explanation: 'Puede ser que' requires subjunctive. Possibility about better timing.

items.question: Probablemente no _____ suficiente tiempo para completar todo el trabajo pendiente.
items.translation: (There probably isn't enough time to complete all the pending work)
items.explanation: 'Probablemente' with negative can use subjunctive. Likely insufficiency with doubt.

items.question: Seguro que _____ olvidado nuestra cita porque no ha aparecido todavía.
items.translation: (I'm sure he's forgotten our appointment because he hasn't shown up yet)
items.explanation: 'Seguro que' with high certainty uses indicative. Confident assumption about forgetting.

items.question: Posiblemente _____ cometido un error al tomar esa decisión tan apresurada.
items.translation: (I possibly made a mistake in making that very hasty decision)
items.explanation: 'Posiblemente' with past uses present perfect subjunctive. Uncertain past error.

items.question: Aunque _____ mucho dinero ahorrado, vive de manera muy modesta y sencilla.
items.translation: (Although he has a lot of money saved, he lives in a very modest and simple way - FACT)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with indicative states known fact. Speaker knows he has money, states it as fact.

items.question: Aunque _____ millonario y muy rico, no cambiaría mi forma de vida actual.
items.translation: (Even if I were a millionaire and very rich, I wouldn't change my current lifestyle - HYPOTHETICAL)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with subjunctive for hypothetical. Not a millionaire, imagining if I were.

items.question: Aunque me _____ toda la noche sin dormir, terminaré este proyecto a tiempo.
items.translation: (Even if I have to stay up all night, I'll finish this project on time)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with future hypothetical uses subjunctive. Uncertain if staying up will be necessary.

items.question: Aunque _____ cansado ayer por la noche, fui al gimnasio como siempre hago.
items.translation: (Although I was tired last night, I went to the gym as I always do - FACT)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with known past fact uses imperfect. Speaker was definitely tired.

items.question: Iré a trabajar mañana aunque _____ enfermo con fiebre alta y malestar.
items.translation: (I'll go to work tomorrow even if I'm sick with high fever and malaise)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with future uncertainty uses subjunctive. Don't know if will be sick tomorrow.

items.question: Aunque me lo _____ muchas veces repetidamente, no logro entenderlo bien todavía.
items.translation: (Although you've explained it to me many times repeatedly, I still can't understand it well - FACT)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with factual past uses present perfect indicative. Known repeated explanations.

items.question: Aunque _____ lloviendo torrencialmente afuera, tenemos que salir ahora mismo urgentemente.
items.translation: (Even though it's pouring rain outside, we have to leave right now urgently - FACT)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with observable fact uses indicative. Currently raining, known fact.

items.question: Aunque _____ a llover de repente, no cancelaremos el evento al aire libre.
items.translation: (Even if it starts to rain suddenly, we won't cancel the outdoor event)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with possible future uses subjunctive. Rain may or may not happen.

items.question: Aunque no me _____ mucho ese género musical, iré al concierto contigo.
items.translation: (Although I don't like that musical genre very much, I'll go to the concert with you - FACT)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' stating personal fact uses indicative. Known preference stated as fact.

items.question: Aunque me _____ un millón de euros, no dejaría de trabajar en mi profesión.
items.translation: (Even if they gave me a million euros, I wouldn't stop working in my profession)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with hypothetical offer uses imperfect subjunctive. Imagining unlikely scenario.

items.question: Aunque _____ llovido mucho anoche, saldremos a caminar por el parque hoy.
items.translation: (Although it rained a lot last night, we'll go out walking through the park today - FACT)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with known past event uses perfect indicative. Definite past rain.

items.question: Aunque _____ todo su esfuerzo máximo, no logró aprobar el examen difícil.
items.translation: (Although he put in all his maximum effort, he didn't manage to pass the difficult exam - FACT)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with known past action uses preterite. Factual past effort stated.

items.question: Aunque me _____ disculpas sinceras ahora, no te perdonaré tan fácilmente.
items.translation: (Even if you apologize sincerely to me now, I won't forgive you so easily)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with hypothetical apology uses subjunctive. Uncertain if apology will come.

items.question: Aunque siempre _____ tarde habitualmente, lo aprecio mucho como amigo leal.
items.translation: (Although he always arrives late habitually, I appreciate him a lot as a loyal friend - FACT)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with habitual fact uses indicative. Known pattern of tardiness.

items.question: Aunque _____ cien años viviendo aquí, nunca me cansaría de este lugar hermoso.
items.translation: (Even if I lived a hundred years here, I would never tire of this beautiful place)
items.explanation: 'Aunque' with hypothetical duration uses imperfect subjunctive. Imagining extended timeframe.

items.question: Conozco a una persona que _____ hablar siete idiomas diferentes con fluidez.
items.translation: (I know a person who can speak seven different languages fluently - DEFINITE)
items.explanation: Known specific person uses indicative. Identified individual with known ability.

items.question: Busco una persona que _____ hablar chino mandarín para un proyecto internacional.
items.translation: (I'm looking for a person who can speak Mandarin Chinese for an international project - INDEFINITE)
items.explanation: Unknown sought person uses subjunctive. Not sure if such person exists.

items.question: Tengo un coche que _____ perfecto para viajes largos por carretera de vacaciones.
items.translation: (I have a car that's perfect for long road trips on vacation - DEFINITE)
items.explanation: Possessed specific car uses indicative. Known car with identified quality.

items.question: Quiero comprar un coche que _____ económico en combustible y espacioso también.
items.translation: (I want to buy a car that's economical in fuel and also spacious - INDEFINITE)
items.explanation: Desired but unidentified car uses subjunctive. Dream car not yet found.

items.question: Hay un restaurante aquí cerca que _____ comida vegetariana orgánica excelente.
items.translation: (There's a restaurant nearby that serves excellent organic vegetarian food - DEFINITE)
items.explanation: 'Hay' with known restaurant uses indicative. Specific identified establishment.

items.question: ¿Hay algún restaurante por aquí que _____ comida italiana auténtica de calidad?
items.translation: (Is there any restaurant around here that serves quality authentic Italian food? - QUESTION)
items.explanation: Question about unknown restaurant uses subjunctive. Uncertain if such place exists.

items.question: El libro que me _____ tu hermano ayer es muy interesante y bien escrito.
items.translation: (The book your brother lent me yesterday is very interesting and well-written - DEFINITE)
items.explanation: Specific known book uses preterite indicative. Definite past lending.

items.question: Necesito alguien que me _____ un libro bueno sobre historia medieval europea.
items.translation: (I need someone who can lend me a good book about European medieval history - INDEFINITE)
items.explanation: Unknown helpful person uses subjunctive. Seeking someone with capability.

items.question: No conozco a nadie que _____ mejor que ella ese deporte tan difícil.
items.translation: (I don't know anyone who plays that very difficult sport better than she does - NEGATIVE)
items.explanation: Negative antecedent 'nadie' requires subjunctive. Denying existence of superior player.

items.question: Vivimos en un barrio que _____ todos los servicios necesarios muy cerca.
items.translation: (We live in a neighborhood that has all necessary services very close - DEFINITE)
items.explanation: Current residence with known features uses indicative. Actual neighborhood characteristics.

items.question: Queremos mudarnos a un barrio que _____ cerca de buenas escuelas públicas.
items.translation: (We want to move to a neighborhood that's close to good public schools - INDEFINITE)
items.explanation: Desired but unidentified neighborhood uses subjunctive. Ideal location not yet found.

items.question: El médico que me _____ ayer es muy profesional y experimentado en su campo.
items.translation: (The doctor who treated me yesterday is very professional and experienced in his field - DEFINITE)
items.explanation: Specific doctor from past uses preterite indicative. Known past treatment.

items.question: Necesito un médico que _____ especializado en medicina deportiva para atletas.
items.translation: (I need a doctor who is specialized in sports medicine for athletes - INDEFINITE)
items.explanation: Seeking unknown specialist uses subjunctive. Uncertain if finding such doctor.

items.question: Trabajo en una empresa que _____ oportunidades excelentes de desarrollo profesional.
items.translation: (I work at a company that offers excellent professional development opportunities - DEFINITE)
items.explanation: Current employer with known benefits uses indicative. Actual workplace features.

items.question: Busco trabajo en una empresa que _____ buen equilibrio entre vida y trabajo.
items.translation: (I'm looking for work at a company that values good work-life balance - INDEFINITE)
items.explanation: Job search for unknown company uses subjunctive. Ideal employer not yet identified.

items.question: _____ lo que _____, no voy a cambiar mi decisión final y definitiva.
items.translation: (Whatever happens, I'm not going to change my final and definitive decision)
items.explanation: 'Pase lo que pase' fixed expression. Whatever may happen uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ quien _____, tendrá que seguir las normas establecidas de la empresa.
items.translation: (Whoever it may be, will have to follow the established company rules)
items.explanation: 'Sea quien sea' fixed expression. Unknown identity uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ donde _____, siempre recordaré con cariño mi ciudad natal querida.
items.translation: (Wherever I am, I'll always remember my beloved hometown with affection)
items.explanation: 'Esté donde esté' fixed expression. Wherever location uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ como _____, debemos aceptar las consecuencias de nuestras acciones pasadas.
items.translation: (Be that as it may, we must accept the consequences of our past actions)
items.explanation: 'Sea como sea' fixed expression. Regardless of circumstances uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ lo que me _____, seguiré adelante con mis planes sin rendirme nunca.
items.translation: (Whatever they say to me, I'll continue forward with my plans without ever giving up)
items.explanation: 'Digan lo que digan' fixed expression. Whatever people say uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ lo que _____, no pienso cambiar de opinión sobre este tema importante.
items.translation: (No matter what you think, I don't plan to change my opinion about this important topic)
items.explanation: 'Pienses lo que pienses' fixed expression. Regardless of opinion uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ lo que _____, la vida continúa y debemos seguir adelante con esperanza.
items.translation: (Whatever happens, life continues and we must go forward with hope)
items.explanation: 'Ocurra lo que ocurra' fixed expression. Whatever events uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ la hora que _____, siempre puedes llamarme si necesitas ayuda urgente.
items.translation: (Whatever time it is, you can always call me if you need urgent help)
items.explanation: 'Sea la hora que sea' fixed expression. Anytime availability uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ donde _____, te encontraré porque te conozco muy bien desde hace años.
items.translation: (Wherever you hide, I'll find you because I've known you very well for years)
items.explanation: 'Te escondas donde te escondas' fixed expression. Wherever hiding uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ duro que _____ el camino, nunca me rendiré ante los obstáculos difíciles.
items.translation: (However hard the path may be, I'll never give up in the face of difficult obstacles)
items.explanation: 'Sea duro que sea' fixed expression. However difficult uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ temprano que _____, siempre hay tráfico pesado en esta autopista congestionada.
items.translation: (However early you leave, there's always heavy traffic on this congested highway)
items.explanation: 'Salgas temprano que salgas' fixed pattern. However early uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ las razones que _____, no jusifican ese comportamiento inaceptable suyo.
items.translation: (Whatever the reasons may be, they don't justify that unacceptable behavior of his)
items.explanation: 'Sean las razones que sean' fixed expression. Whatever justifications use double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ el precio que _____, voy a comprar ese regalo especial para mi madre.
items.translation: (Whatever the price is, I'm going to buy that special gift for my mother)
items.explanation: 'Sea el precio que sea' fixed expression. Regardless of cost uses double subjunctive.

items.question: _____ el motivo que _____, no deberías haber actuado de esa manera tan grosera.
items.translation: (Whatever the motive was, you shouldn't have acted in such a rude manner)
items.explanation: 'Fuera el motivo que fuera' past fixed expression. Whatever past reason uses imperfect subjunctive.

items.question: _____ tarde que _____, siempre me espera despierto hasta que llego a casa.
items.translation: (However late I arrive, he always waits for me awake until I get home)
items.explanation: 'Llegue tarde que llegue' fixed pattern. However late uses double subjunctive.
